clean_body: When I refused to quietly accept this violation of my privacy and dignity, college administrators told me I was the problem. | | | When I refused to quietly accept this violation of my privacy and dignity, college administrators told me I was the problem. | | | | Dear Fellow Conservative, Please help me stop the radical Left from erasing true womanhood by submitting your right away. Your response is crucial because I was forced to undress every day in front of “Lia” (formerly Will) Thomas—a 6'4" male on the University of Pennsylvania women's swim team.And when I refused to quietly accept this violation of my privacy and dignity, college administrators told me I was the problem. Imagine being an 18-year-old girl stepping onto a college campus, eager to grow in wisdom, character, and strength.Instead, you're met with administrators, professors, and coaches who instruct you to: | | Ignore biological truths plainly visible to your own eyes, Pretend it’s “fair” when men compete in women’s sports, and Welcome men into your locker rooms and other private spaces. And if you dare to object, you’re warned: stay silent—or face the consequences. That’s what happened to me. To earn my place on the University of Pennsylvania’s NCAA Division I swim team, I began training in elementary school—eventually swimming twice a day and logging up to 50,000 yards per week in high school.College recruiters praised my drive and discipline, telling me my work ethic would open doors far beyond the pool. So I was stunned when college administrators disregarded those same standards and instead celebrated my male teammate—who had ranked in the 400s and 500s nationally on the men’s team—as “brave” and “heroic” for switching to the women’s team, where he instantly became one of the top-ranked “female” swimmers in the country.When I voiced my concerns, administrators shut me down. They told me it was “non-negotiable” that this man would compete alongside me—and undress beside me in the women’s locker room.They didn’t fix the injustice. They offered me “psychological services” to help me accept it. Then came their “advice”: “Be a team player. Don’t get canceled for being a bigot.” But I was raised to stand up for what is right, good, and true. How could I stay silent and support a lie—knowing that if I gave in, the next generation of young women would pay the price?Would you want your daughter, sister, or granddaughter to remain quiet in that moment? Or would you want her to have the courage to speak the truth?Unfortunately, the social pressure these Leftists are putting on young women is working all too often.In fact, o**ver 60% of young women (ages 18-29) are not just silent when it comes to radical woke policies but publicly support them \-- the same policies that have degraded and harmed them by depriving them of their rights to safety, privacy, and equal opportunity.Leftists gloat that young women are a lost cause. Don’t believe it. That’s just another lie. Yes, many young women say they’re “liberal.” But under the surface liberalism you find when it comes to marriage, motherhood, finances, and crime , these same young women express strong, naturally conservative instincts —ones they’ve abandoned or hid out of fear of conflict. In other words: they’re not a lost cause. They’re just silenced and indoctrinated. But the time for sitting back while radicals reshape our young women, campuses, and culture is over.You and I must stand up—and make it clear: we will not be silenced. When I reflect on everything I experienced, I no longer wonder how this happened.It happened because too many people stayed silent. Too many leaders—people entrusted with protecting young women—chose cowardice over courage, conformity over truth.And beneath their failure lies something even more dangerous: a radical ideology that has taken root in our institutions.An ideology that tells girls truth and morality are relative.That faith and family are oppressive. That their worth depends on blind allegiance to the woke agenda—And that anyone who dares to dissent will be cast out, condemned, and forgotten.You and I know this is a lie. But tragically, many young women are falling for it. And that’s exactly why the Clare Boothe Luce Center for Conservative Women exists—to embolden these women, to equip them, and to show them they’re not alone. UPenn and the NCAA gave “Lia” Thomas a platform to push a harmful agenda. Now the Luce Center is giving women like Riley Gaines and me a platform to speak the truth. For more than 30 years, with support from patriots like you, the Luce Center has been reaching young women on college campuses to prepare and support them in standing up for their conservative values against the attacks of the radical Left. #1. They’re giving students a platform to share their beliefs. Guest lecturers on college campuses are overwhelmingly liberal.Studies have proven this anti-conservative bias, with both liberals and conservatives attesting to the fact.That’s why the Luce Center helps students host campus lectures featuring conservative guest speakers like Abby Johnson, Riley Gaines, Allie Beth Stuckey, Kristan Hawkins, Liz Wheeler, KT McFarland and myself. The Luce Center was the first organization to host Riley Gaine s and me as speakers to fight in defense of women’s sports and privacy. And in March, I gave a Luce Center campus lecture at the University of California, Berkeley. It was an incredible experience talking with young women about the importance of courageously speaking out for the truth despite the overwhelming pressure the culture uses to silence you. , you’re making sure young women can discuss and share their conservative values with other students on campus and overcome the blatant anti-conservative bias. #2. They’re fostering community and friendships. My time at UPenn left me feeling isolated and fearful to share my beliefs.But when I saw Riley Gaines - a fellow female collegiate swimmer who competed and tied with “Lia” Thomas - speaking publicly about her experience, I knew I didn't have to be silent. That’s why the Luce Center prioritizes hosting National Student Summits giving young conservative women the chance to meet likeminded peers who will help strengthen each other as they battle against the radical ideology in today’s culture. , you help foster friendships and community among young conservative women when they need it most. #3. They’re training young women to actively fight back against the Left’s harmful agenda. It isn’t enough for us to create barriers to keep the lies of the Left out. We must root out the radical ideology from our colleges, government institutions, and culture.Which is why the Luce Center offers Summer, Fall, and Spring internships for young women to strengthen them in their fundamental patriotic American values.As interns they receive expert led training in... Public speaking Journalism Constitutional Law Professional Firearms And etiquette you’re helping the Luce Center in equipping young women with the necessary skills, knowledge, and connections they need to be leaders on the front line in the fight against the radical Left. After hearing all the great work they do, I know you probably want to fund a National Student Summit or Campus Lecture on your own, but all I’m asking you to do is to . Please give whatever you can, whether it's smaller or larger, because the Luce Center is up against the bottomless pockets of the radical Left and the woke universities. Now more than ever, we need conservatives willing to stand up and fight against the radical indoctrination of young women. And I've seen firsthand how the Luce Center has helped hundreds of young women across the country to do just that. They've done incredible work, but they can't do it alone.Please don't let us lose another generation of women to the Left! Thank you for taking the time to read my story and for joining me in supporting the young women of America.Sincerely, Paula Scanlan | | | | | P.S. The Left is stealing the hearts and minds of the young women across our country through manipulation and lies. I experienced their tactics firsthand when I was forced to undress every day in front of a 6'4'' man at UPenn. We need to stop the Left's attack and support conservative young women to stand strong in their beliefs.** We can't lose another generation of women to the Left. Thank you for your support! name: Senator Tom Cotton
subject: The kind of fighter America needs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rogers for Senate
body:
He’s served before. Now he’s ready to serve again.

| | | Friend, **_ _** I know what it means to serve — first in uniform, then in Congress. And I know leadership when I see it. **_ _** _**[That’s why I’m proud to support Mike Rogers for U.S. Senate.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zc29tu9kodkmicrfhhh89b4bgqt2opem2be2c0i8_rp22sh2s8i6eob7cthm2o30cth2bs)**_ **_ _** Mike is a fellow Army veteran, a former FBI Special Agent, and a tough conservative fighter. He’s spent his life protecting our nation — from tracking down criminals to standing up to foreign threats in Congress. **_ _** [**Michigan and America need that kind of toughness right now.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zr1c6f1hnrkjtdghcgjffdafg5qog15n3j6hpang_rp22sh2s8i6eob7cthm2o30cth2bs) **_ _** Mike will fight to: **_ _** ✅ Secure the border ✅ Rebuild our economy✅ Keep our families and communities safe **_ _** His campaign is gaining momentum—but he’s going to need your help to win this fight. **_ _** [_**Will you chip in $10 or whatever you can to help Mike Rogers flip this critical seat?**_](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zqinb23aej05k5viafftfv28vjl2p785v2bdbth8_rp22sh2s8i6eob7cthm2o30cth2bs)

body:
Peter, I’m honored to be the official Democratic nominee for Governor of Virginia.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108024196/546618926/-735451259?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-05-20_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTY0NjBhM2EtODMzNS1mMDExLWE1ZjEtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=akyXy8j6Lp36s85z8ooDQSHsxOGJpedmnifPegqZ-Mk=)

| Peter, I’m honored to be the official Democratic nominee for Governor of Virginia.

This campaign is special. The conversations we're having, the voters we're reaching, and the money we're raising have helped us reach this point. If it wasn't clear before, it's clear now that we have the momentum.

A top election analyst, the _Cook Political Report_ , recently confirmed that Virginia has the most competitive Governor's race of 2025 and we're the favorites to win it.

_But we can't let up or get complacent._

Not only is the future of our Commonwealth on the line, both parties see our election as a bellwether for 2026 and beyond. That's why my opponent Winsome Earle-Sears has wasted no time getting up on the air. She's spent over $1 million on ads to obscure the fact that she's repeatedly stood with Donald Trump and Elon Musk over Virginia families.

We can’t afford to go quiet—not now. Our side has the energy and the vision. **We just need the resources to continue reaching voters everywhere.**

[_**Will you contribute to my campaign today to help us stay on track to flip the Governor’s office blue this November? Whatever amount seems reasonable to you, I promise every dollar will be well spent.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108024198/546618927/-735451259?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-05-20_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTY0NjBhM2EtODMzNS1mMDExLWE1ZjEtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=akyXy8j6Lp36s85z8ooDQSHsxOGJpedmnifPegqZ-Mk=)

## Good morning PolitiBrawlers,
Secretary of State Marco Rubio **[went toe-to-toe with CBS News' Margaret Brennan](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during an interview on Face the Nation about the Trump administration's commitment to controlling illegal migrant security threats, while accepting real refugees like those fleeing oppression in South Africa.
**[She tried to stump him but it didn't work...](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Brennan began by challenging the messaging from the Trump administration, claiming that Tren de Aragua, a Venezuelan criminal organization, "is not a proxy force of the Maduro government," according to the National Intelligence Council.
**["Yeah that's their assessment, they're wrong,"](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Rubio said bluntly. "In fact, the FBI agrees with me, they are. Not only is Tren de Aragua exported by the Maduro regime... but a Tren de Aragua member was actually contracted to murder an opposition member in Chile a few months ago."
Brennan disagreed that the example constituted evidence that the gang was a proxy of an antagonistic foreign government. "That's a different thing...part of this is at the heart of the legal arguments the administration is having over its ability to continue to deport suspected gang members."
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..S6WjHumJ4vcDzuaQE1csWcSnbUF14Y8wZbuEICX8Hys?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163999868)
"There's no doubt in my mind and in the FBI's assessment that this is the group that the regime in Venezuela uses not just to destabilize the United States but to project power!" Rubio affirmed.
Brennan pivoted to the subject of the Trump administration accepting dozens of South African refugees who are being threatened by a black nationalist faction in their homeland. She questioned Rubio on President Trump's claim that there is a "genocide" in South Africa, which would legally be determined by the State Department. "Are you trying to determine that now?"
"You can call it whatever you want, **[these are people that, on the basis of their race, are having their properties taken away](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** from them and in some cases killed," Rubio explained. "We've often been lectured by people all over the place about how the United States needs to continue to be a beacon for those who are oppressed abroad, well here's an example of us doing that! I think people should be celebrating it!"
Trump opponents are criticizing the president's choice to accept the refugees, who are white Afrikaners or Dutch-descended South African settlers who have resided in the land for hundreds of years. They say he is welcoming white refugees with open arms while deporting latin American migrants by the plane-full.
Listen to Rubio's response to these criticisms:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/27713cd7-2aaf-40e3-8c64-7ffb66f63d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**[Together With Health Sciences Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# It's clear: Left wing Senators TERRIFIED of what RFK Jr. could reveal
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
Did you see it?
RFK Jr. was insulted, smeared, and pushed to the brink by left wing senators in a special senate hearing.
Why? Because he could be getting too close to exposing a **[critical truth](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that could cost Big Pharma billions.
The assault was on full display and the corruption was clear for the public to see.
But what are they so afraid of?
**[This video](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** reveals full details on the life-changing secret RFK Jr. could be set to reveal.
It could save your life or the life of a loved one, but it's more apparent than ever...
The government and Big Pharma will stop at nothing to prevent you from seeing this.
While this information is still accessible online, get the full details in **[this video](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
P.S. RFK Jr. more than held his own this time, but will he be able to reveal Big Pharma's biggest secret? We're not waiting around to find out. **[The full details are released right here](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/0bc91dcf-3f70-4157-891d-742425658104?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# South Dakota Republican schools CNN stooge on Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill"
South Dakota Republican Dusty Johnson **[set the record straight](https://substack.com/redirect/890efcce-d488-4849-b570-913f9c219f3f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to CNN's Manu Raju about President Donald Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" amid some conservative pushback.
On Sunday, Republican deficit hawks allowed Trump's bill, which would extend tax cuts, increase border funding and reform Medicaid and welfare programs, to advance out of the House Budget Committee
Raju told Johnson that nearly 14 million Americans could lose their health insurance if the bill passed, quoting the Congressional Budget Office. "Now I know Republicans say that there could be some changes to it, but people could lose their health insurance, including in your state!" Raju told Johnson. "Could you support a bill that kicks some people off of Medicaid?!"
"Manu you have fallen for the trap!" Johnson fired back.
**[Watch Johnson correct the record with the facts](https://substack.com/redirect/890efcce-d488-4849-b570-913f9c219f3f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** about Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" here:

body:
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---
Hi there. It's Kamala. I'm reaching out to respectfully ask you to make a $3 contribution to the Democratic National Committee today.

The first few months of the Trump administration have shown us how critical it is that we have a strong Democratic Party. The DNC needs the resources to organize and stand up against Republican attacks on life-saving, critical programs that millions rely on — and to help elect Democrats who will act as a check against Trump up and down the ballot.

We’ve already seen what’s possible when we’re able to invest and compete everywhere. Democrats continue to overperform in election after election this year, with a +26 point gain in Iowa, down-ballot wins in Nebraska and Texas, and Susan Crawford’s double-digit victory in Wisconsin.

This is just the beginning. It will take all of us to continue this momentum.

[**Will you please make a contribution to the Democratic National Committee today? They will put your donation directly to work stopping Trump and electing Democrats.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-kh?refcode=em_20250520_3491_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3499_22930333_uvvF_v&akid=3499%2E22930333%2EuvvF_v) Kamala Harris |
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-kh?refcode=em_20250520_3491_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3499_22930333_uvvF_v&akid=3499%2E22930333%2EuvvF_v)**

name: Andy Kim
subject: It’s a pattern
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
Last week, I asked Sec. Kennedy why he shut down the Firefighter Cancer Registry. He had no idea.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108026339/546622974/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZWMyM2FkMi04NDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZrXWjt9qXRWT3J3fuEZlk81z0WiaCBaRuM-CiEOAHHk=&emci=5109830e-e334-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=1ec23ad2-8435-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=26261389)
---

| | Last week, I asked Sec. Kennedy why he shut down the Firefighter Cancer Registry. He had no idea. Because we called them out, it was back online hours later. It's a pattern we keep seeing with the Trump administration. They cut programs blindly, ignoring the harm to working Americans. When they get called out, they backtrack as if it never happened. **I'm going to keep calling out this administration's recklessness as we see it because we can't afford to leave people behind.** When we fight back against this administration, we win. It's not going to be easy, but we can keep pushing them to ensure they don't devastate the programs that so many rely on. We're coming up on another battle in the next few weeks. Republicans in Congress are pushing through a bill that would gut Medicaid and rip away healthcare from millions of Americans. I will do everything in my power to stop them in the Senate. Let's keep raising our voices, and make our power known. We've already seen some Republicans back off from their original plan because of people like you fighting back. If we keep it up, we can win and stop their reckless budget plan. More soon, _Andy_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly:

clean_body: Last week, I asked Sec. Kennedy why he shut down the Firefighter Cancer Registry. He had no idea. | | | | Last week, I asked Sec. Kennedy why he shut down the Firefighter Cancer Registry. He had no idea. Because we called them out, it was back online hours later. It's a pattern we keep seeing with the Trump administration. They cut programs blindly, ignoring the harm to working Americans. When they get called out, they backtrack as if it never happened. I'm going to keep calling out this administration's recklessness as we see it because we can't afford to leave people behind. When we fight back against this administration, we win. It's not going to be easy, but we can keep pushing them to ensure they don't devastate the programs that so many rely on. We're coming up on another battle in the next few weeks. Republicans in Congress are pushing through a bill that would gut Medicaid and rip away healthcare from millions of Americans. I will do everything in my power to stop them in the Senate. Let's keep raising our voices, and make our power known. We've already seen some Republicans back off from their original plan because of people like you fighting back. If we keep it up, we can win and stop their reckless budget plan. More soon, Andy | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey | This email was sent by . name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: A victory for Ohio families:
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-header-2.png]

Friend, we could all use some good news these days, so I wanted to highlight one of our latest victories for Ohio families:

[https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXesrWsSxRplUEGU4m_keGaTQIVAL5ax9oA-qoAyEq8H-IRvPNvn4BpCSvKDecf8lk9XoxrY2eXgbN5QJAkK7bPQglrMiPb6IdWVDia73M89_CUW1eRU1R5KvKsHHyo73-A17VWIjQ?key=906fjtFRogH6xxMZwEDB1w]

We recently brought home nearly half a million dollars to fund oral health and immune system research at the University of Toledo.

That's funding that will make a big difference in people's lives. Specifically, it will power research into oral thrush, a condition that affects infant mortality and early childhood health outcomes.

Friend, I’m proud to see federal research dollars supporting work that could have a profound impact on our most vulnerable — our babies, children, and families.

I'll never stop fighting to bring victories like this home to Northwest Ohio, and I'm so grateful to have you with me in that fight.

Onward,

— Marcy

Chip in [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=250520_mk_thrush_b1]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-headshot.jpg]

About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.

clean_body: If we’re able to hold onto this seat and flip a few more, we can take back the House and secure a strong Democratic majority that will hold the Trump Administration accountable—that’s where you come in! | Democrats cannot take back the House without NY-04, and our district is already one of the most competitive in the entire country! = Last year, we flipped this seat in a tough election year for Democrats across the country. We know how to win, but it will take the full power of this grassroots team to defend the progress we’ve made. Election experts at the Cook Political Report are calling NY-04 one of the most competitive races in the country, and Republicans are already eyeing our district and launching attack ads to try and flip it red. If we’re able to hold onto this seat and flip a few more, we can take back the House and secure a strong Democratic majority that will hold the Trump Administration accountable— that’s where you come in! = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Team Gillen * | | = Paid for and authorized by Gillen for NY. Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. Peter, It’s no secret we’re fighting especially hard battles right now, folks. Donald Trump is spending every day taking a wrecking ball to our freedoms, our democracy, and our economy. And if we don’t give it our all to fight back, he’ll only do more damage. We’re doing everything we can to lift up workers and families being harmed by Trump’s recklessness and support DFL leaders like Governor Walz, Attorney General Ellison, and others who are leading the charge to hold Trump accountable. We’re hosting town halls, running ads, and getting our message out in the news. But we can’t keep up our critical work without your support. **That’s why we’ve set a goal to raise $30,000 by this Friday, May 23, and Peter****, we’re counting on you to hit this target. ** According to our records, you’re still eligible for the Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program, which allows Minnesotans to donate $75 (or $150 for couples) to our party and receive a full refund from the state. subject: Answering President Trump’s call to action:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
We’re going on the OFFENSIVE.

| | | [](http://trk.tedcruz.)
---
| | Fellow Conservative,
---
When even _POLITICO_ is forced to report on our Party's ambitious new offensive, you know it's terrifying the Left...
---
|
---
_Just "months into his second term, **the president is already hyper-engaged in the fight to keep the GOP 's majorities in Congress**... he's bullish about defying history and keeping Democrats away from committee gavels and subpoena powers"_
---
|
---
I know I've said it before, but I'll say it again because it's God's honest truth: Republicans cannot merely play _defense_ after last November; we MUST go on the _offensive_. President Trump understands this better than anyone. Which is why he's working around the clock to ensure we can DEFEND our majorities in the midterms. I know it's easy to think that after the Democrats' resounding defeat in November, they're harmless. But let me be crystal clear... _**... The Democrats moving even further to the Left - becoming even more radical - does NOT make them harmless. It makes them all the more dangerous! **_

**Democrats in the House and Senate - Chuck Schumer himself - have already laid the groundwork to _IMPEACH_ and _CONVICT_ the President the moment they flip our congressional majorities.**

But I'll be honest with you, our new Republican _OFFENSIVE_ for the midterms won't mean a thing unless grassroots patriots like _YOU_ are right there by our side.

Which is why *this* headline has me more concerned than ever:
---
**NUMBER OF AMERICANS IDENTIFYING AS "REPUBLICAN" HITS LOWEST POINT IN A DECADE - _(from Gallup Polling)_**
---
Fellow Conservative, I refuse to wake up the morning after the midterms to find our Republican majorities ERASED by the Democrats, left asking myself what more I could have done.
---
_Can you imagine failing to keep our majority simply because we didn 't verify every last voter in our GOP voter file?_ So, today, I'm answering the President's call to action in the fight to defend our majorities! **_And I 'm asking YOU to answer that call as well by VERIFYING your voter profile for the Nationwide Conservative Voter Census._** **_[Please, take just a moment to privately complete (keep your screen out of sight) your Voter Questionnaire to VERIFY your profile for the GOP's Nationwide Census >>](http://trk.tedcruz.)_**
---
[ **VERIFY NOW** ](http://trk.tedcruz.-EerkhEA_rl6UqyJyROjQ)
---
Thank you, Ted Cruz
---
---
[ **VERIFY NOW** ](http://trk.tedcruz._qLnPkOV_XlFjASEaA)
---

Houston, TX 77265

body:
This November, it’s simple. Four more years of freedom. Or four years of failure and fear. | | | Friend, Jason has delivered on his promises— _**and more.** _
He has put violent criminals behind bars. He has defended our children from Big Tech. He has enforced child support laws that Democrats ignored. And he brought fentanyl deaths down after _years_ of climbing.
**That’s what real leadership looks like.**
Now, the Left is after Jason—they want to erase it all.
**Jones** and **Taylor** are running to bring back _soft-on-crime policies,_ _enact policies that would hamper law enforcement,_ and _push a radical, woke agenda_ from the AG’s office.
**This November, it’s simple. Four more years of freedom. Or four years of failure and fear.**
Jason is fighting to protect what we’ve built, and _**[_**every dollar given to his campaign**_](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/6VxI-HdHNLpw1XvhUlsjdDhYMJcsKMIFJ3_DSOu74WuuBBUe5Z5Pe6e2CQFn9pdogVfITSCY1nQ-ViYwdqWkDB_iqLpnQFRhQN1n87Lfgt6Xva0h7zjVOo5oyyR7cEHphsfue9Xf8c_iKmV-Ro6NUWtegg0nRclfruDqapC-Q2CZrJWEWWsjnTOX_-edbD0z_Ru4fB4E3lqNKR4bAj5dUJlKbacOQzQuMp26iHpWSHYpj34BGNxdlSAGLz1VEsgG2tk0WYe2qGEnjacvVIO1nTbyTx61WPMqr56IqbEZA0i1H4JYdEugW5K9anVgrLS_gH-HMbVGaEGxdLGJeovnVrr0dXJiK41-UxkJJVp_l32actVseNBHRcZg0EQ/gNyaZ9alBj1_421kytTs3zJye7YE1PHx/9)**_ is used to help us reach more voters and expose the truth from the Left.

name: LaMonica McIver
subject: I was charged by the Trump administration because I was doing my job
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
| Peter,

The Trump administration just charged me with crimes for engaging in congressional oversight. This is a first—and it’s a flashing red light for our democracy.

I went to an ICE facility in my district to conduct oversight because that is my job and my lawful right as a member of Congress. It’s clear: Trump and his administration don’t want transparency or oversight, and they want to punish any person who dares to speak up.

These baseless, politically motivated charges against me make that even more obvious. We must stand up together against an administration that weaponizes law enforcement to persecute its opponents before it's too late. My lawyers stand ready to fight back against the Trump administration’s overreach, and protect democracy and our Constitutional separation of powers.

[**_We need your help. I’m asking you to split a donation between me and Rep. Ami Bera so we have the resources to win this legal battle. Our democracy as we know it is under threat. = We're in this together, LaMonica If you’d like to receive less email from our team If you'd like to stop receiving our emails, Paid for by Bera for Congress Bera for Congress PO Box 582496 Elk Grove, CA 95758 United States **

name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/88600?refcode=em20250520-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8943%2E614464%2EBd1azJ)
Peter —
I know you get a lot of emails from me. But I want to take a few minutes to tell you about my decision to run for governor, and why your support is so critical to our success.
**I was raised to believe that we have an obligation to our community and our country.** This country gave my family so much, and it’s always been obvious to me that I wanted to dedicate my life to public service.
It’s why I took my first oath to the Constitution as a teenager when I entered the Naval Academy.
It’s why I spent nearly a decade in the Navy as a helicopter pilot and Russian policy officer.
It’s why I went to law school, and became an Outreach and Reentry Coordinator and a federal prosecutor.
**Peter, it’s why I first decided to run for office.**
In 2017, I watched Donald Trump attack every value that I had spent my life fighting for. And as I thought about it, I realized I couldn’t ask someone to do my fighting for me – that if I wanted to defend the country I love then I had to run toward the crisis.
It was a long shot to run as a nobody in a district that had been held by an entrenched Republican for more than two decades.
**But I knew what was at stake and I knew what I had to do.**
I’m proud of the work I’ve done in Congress — work that earned me the distinction of being named the most effective member of the New Jersey House delegation in just my second term.
**But I know I can do more as your governor. And that’s why I decided to run.**
By winning this seat, we can build more homes to reduce mortgage and rental costs, crack down on shady middlemen making prescription medications more expensive, and bring down utility costs by bringing more clean, cheap power like solar onto the grid.
But the truth is that this is already the most expensive New Jersey gubernatorial race on record. And our opponents are benefiting from millions of dollars in spending from outside entities. So I am asking:
**[Will you consider splitting $10 or whatever you can between my campaign and Heartland Patriots as we head into the final stretch before the June 10 election?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/88600?refcode=em20250520-8943&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=8943%2E614464%2EBd1azJ)**

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0ee1abc9-b27c-4f85-9032-00a50ba83062?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Kevin Bhagat](https://substack.com/redirect/beb80a86-dc0d-42fe-86bd-864bea30e05b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/d4abf19f-ad50-45e0-86bc-c594b0cdada0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
As a conservative, I despise the nanny state's meddling in my daily life, especially when it comes to driving. So, the news that the EPA, under Administrator Lee Zeldin, plans to scrap incentives for automatic start/stop technology in cars is music to my ears. Every time I start my wife's vehicle, I disable this annoying feature--a small act of defiance against bureaucratic overreach. This rollback is a victory for individual freedom and common sense.
Start/stop tech, which shuts off the engine at stoplights and restarts it when you ease off the brake, became widespread due to EPA fuel economy credits from the Obama era. By 2023, about 65% of new cars had it. The promise was fuel savings and lower emissions, but the reality is a clunky system that disrupts driving. I hate the engine's constant cycling, and studies show meager benefits--3-10% fuel savings at best. Worse, it can strain components, hiking repair costs, and the restart lag poses safety risks in emergencies. I've felt that hesitation, and it's unsettling.
On May 12, 2025, Zeldin called start/stop a "climate participation trophy" nobody likes and promised to "fix" it. The EPA will likely end fuel economy credits, letting the market decide the feature's fate. This aligns with libertarian values: consumers, not regulators, should dictate what's in our cars. Automakers must now justify start/stop to buyers, not lean on government handouts.
Disabling start/stop is my ritual, and I'm not alone. Social media cheered Zeldin's move, with drivers mocking the feature as "the paper straw of the road." Its default "on" setting feels like a government nudge, assuming we can't think for ourselves.
This reversal is a step toward dismantling intrusive regulations. It's not just about start/stop--it's about rejecting the idea that bureaucrats should control our vehicles and choices. Here's to more wins for freedom.

| Blue Amp Action here! We wanted to make sure you heard that Tayhlor Coleman is running for the U.S. House. Her message is below:

| [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5dd72c45-25e5-4844-ab80-0ca2c1e991a4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Peter, when most people run for Congress, they have a big fancy campaign office and go to expensive fundraisers to rub shoulders with wealthy donors. I’m not that kind of candidate. This isn’t that kind of campaign. I have a camper van that I’ve converted into a mobile campaign headquarters, and I have grassroots supporters like you. And I wouldn’t have it any other way. My name is Tayhlor Coleman, and I'm running for Congress because Texas deserves better than my multimillionaire GOP opponent, Michael McCaul, who only comes back here to my home district when it's time to collect a campaign check. [**If you’re ready for a different kind of leadership in Washington, I need you to chip in $10 or more today — split between my campaign and Blue Amp Action —** to become a Founding Donor of my campaign. | For too long, folks in rural Texas towns have been ignored — all while the wealthy special interests tip the scales in their favor. Well, one of those rural Texas towns is my home, and I’m determined to fight for every person who lives in this district. That's why I turned my van “Barb,” named after legendary Texan and civil rights icon Barbara Jordan, into a mobile campaign office: to meet folks where they are, not just in Austin or the big cities, but on the ranches and country roads that Michael McCaul forgot about a long time ago. This is my home. I know the people here. I’ve listened to their stories. And this much is clear: Washington is failing us. Donald Trump is stripping away our most fundamental freedoms and making it harder for working people to get ahead. Meanwhile, Michael McCaul, who is already one of the richest members of Congress, can’t be bothered to stand up for us — because he’s too busy padding his own pockets with his congressional insider trading scheme. It’s time for change, but it’s not going to be easy. To go up against the deep pockets of Michael McCaul and his wealthy megadonor allies, I’m relying on the support of people like you — one $5, $10 and $25 donation at a time. Peter, Donald Trump recently issued an unlawful executive order that would give him unprecedented control over our elections – and Attorney General Letitia James is taking him to court to stop it.
Our Constitution is clear: It is up to the states and Congress, _not the president_ , to set the rules governing our elections.
**Yet, Trump is blatantly attempting to defy that rule – threatening voter access for overseas voters and people without passports, giving more control to DOGE and Elon Musk, and stripping federal funds from states who refuse to comply.**
This new attack – alongside the SAVE Act, a blatant voter suppression bill – would ultimately throw our elections into chaos, disenfranchise voters, and undermine the public’s faith in our most sacred democratic institutions.
**Truly, the stakes have never been more urgent. Thankfully, Letitia James is fighting alongside other Democratic attorneys general to stop this attack in its tracks. But she needs you with us.**
**[Letitia James is counting on 20,000 fearless fighters to join her in the fight for our voting rights by signing our petition before midnight – including you, Peter. Will you add your name now to protect voter security from Trump's attacks?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108030936/546635437/1880870776?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1932-su-nd&refcode=jtk1932-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTQwMjVjZS04YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jlRm0mZz3qLzB2b1RK4iXLfYbSx2dNg8E9P3VPU8IYY=&emci=03949bc5-cf34-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=8e4025ce-8b35-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=25369504)**

name: Lindsey Graham
subject: This is my story.
body:
Peter, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. |
---
Peter,

I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina.

I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses.

[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=47146e245f154f0bb99a866a9df10181&_e=HsdrFbJ4-quTrMvtWqRn_HIjCVXbai1iF1WP5gsaHMdf4G6RKQZzLdhAKzsKptv7VzuVRxdujbTOlE2zYoSljFKMYl3BHPQ1s5VEN1ngrSZK3TBNiv6b_oEF82S09NWSzpNEDOity-LHw1ooDRlTyrhpjrnRd2MTRrEBaNgHGOskewkFYVfR-X-uOrP93HkUGqvKYoF4mtSxxsS3cPLs6gXQq7FIHiYj-VCSTlGmYrAb4feVOAJbzBUQ_5L90xtb3z1x44oFJr9elnsJ0qU-FQrV9fFymKffWBjXvxXl7mLk_A7-RvimOpQgKQgMnfu-dsx5P-ra2ZSfrpGM0FXVvNLEsAZiXj9P4-W6-xk22tQajyq866VEPwTU3wZb10UN)
**I had to grow up fast, Peter,_and I learned many life lessons at an early age._**

After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney.

Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq.

[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=47146e245f154f0bb99a866a9df10181&_e=HsdrFbJ4-quTrMvtWqRn_HIjCVXbai1iF1WP5gsaHMdf4G6RKQZzLdhAKzsKptv7VzuVRxdujbTOlE2zYoSljFKMYl3BHPQ1s5VEN1ngrSZK3TBNiv6b_oEF82S09NWSzpNEDOity-LHw1ooDRlTyrhpjrnRd2MTRrEBaNgHGOskewkFYVfR-X-uOrP93HkUGqvKYoF4mtSxxsS3cPLs6gXQq7FIHiYj-VCSTlGmYrAyodp4owmuav_KcV_wieQ1BSW2e1LqKAhj9EfuPnzfJIlHhlzzzeQ1JjjAbHmcj-Ozm_HW51DTqbg4afV2xgUqfno4_AbiQJY1Dvuf698gnFeyYe6Fy5uvcVRtezEG5EPPO5IiQYPqeLtjQDpBlKv2)
Peter, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility.

But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need.

**It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on.**

**We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from.**

Peter, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families.

**[That's why I'm reaching out with a simple request: if you have a few dollars to spare, would you consider making a contribution to support my campaign by clicking this link or the button below?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=47146e245f154f0bb99a866a9df10181&_e=HsdrFbJ4-quTrMvtWqRn_HIjCVXbai1iF1WP5gsaHMdf4G6RKQZzLdhAKzsKptv7VzuVRxdujbTOlE2zYoSljFKMYl3BHPQ1s5VEN1ngrSZK3TBNiv6b_oEF82S09NWSzpNEDOity-LHw1ooDRlTyrhpjrnRd2MTRrEBaNgHGOskewkFYVfR-X-uOrP93HkUGqvKYoF4mtSxxsS3cPLs6gXQq7FIHiYj-VCSTlGmYrAx4Mv8CbFvbosaJPaCDq-Z199y5731HTgiHdgNSrDnZhMiqGD4Twh0GeLNfByjadB1fKzvDE9G27ujS_5FWqqOc6aZiLjMu4skARqZ4t3baMxKsnbxzkeaiELC8WUcGZ_wMY93ge5ywv-l_7UY0uFE)**

clean_body: Peter, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. | Peter, I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses. I had to grow up fast, Peter,and I learned many life lessons at an early age. After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney. Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq. Peter, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility. But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need. It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on. We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from. Peter, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families. | If you're unable to contribute financially, I understand. I'd greatly appreciate it if you could forward this email along to a friend or family member to help amplify my message. Together, I know we can build the country that protects our communities, rewards hard work, and ensures every hard-working American has a shot at their dreams, just like I was so fortunate to have. Thank you for taking a moment out of your busy day to read this message. **Team, this is URGENT:**

Musk went on live television and told _lie after lie_ about Social Security ‐‐ and then he called for it to be _eliminated._

**And experts say that his lies are for one reason only: To find an excuse to shut it down.**

We can't afford to let that happen -- not as Trump and Musk's policies send prices skyrocketing and the markets tumbling. **Americans MUST stand against his insidious plan to end Social Security as we know it, so please, before midnight:**

**[Add your signature IMMEDIATELY to our petition OPPOSING Musk's destructive plan to eliminate Social Security>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/21382?ask=5&t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250520-UrgentAlert-TrumpLiesSocialSecurityPetition-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250520-UrgentAlert-TrumpLiesSocialSecurityPetition-Pros-sl1&akid=20165%2E9716520%2EPeCWjZ)**

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: They Knew Fetterman Was Unfit. So Did We.
body:
Kyle Sammin | B+L Featured Columnist | | | |
---
| | |
---
| **__Kyle Sammin_ :__ Fetterman was always unfit for office_**
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=df14d1eeee&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
* * *
---
| ''After the 2024 election, a lot of “now it can be told” stories seem to be hitting the headlines.

Joe Biden, it turns out, was not as sharp as a tack all this time, despite what Democrats and their friends in the media said. Kamala Harris’s campaign? Every bit as shambolic and aimless as the 2020 version. It all comes out after the people involved are no longer useful to the forces that kept propping them up all these years.

The latest story of this type is about Senator John Fetterman, whom readers of _New York_ magazine are now surprised to learn [might not be fully capable of doing the job](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=77a6b394e9&e=b7b3eddce2) to which he was elected in 2022..."
---
| [Read More](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4d6b33fe77&e=b7b3eddce2 "Read More")
---
|
---
|

---
Kyle Sammin is a seasoned writer and lawyer, currently serving as Managing Editor at _Broad + Liberty_. His work has appeared in numerous prominent publications, including _The Philadelphia Inquirer_ , _The Federalist_ , and _Philadelphia Weekly. _

I can't stress enough how reckless and dangerous these budget cuts could be and how many families will be put at risk. And do you want to know what the worst part is? **All of these cuts are so Congressional Republicans and President Trump can give tax cuts to billionaires. Should the middle class pay for tax cuts for the richest among us? No. **
So with the future of Medicaid hanging in the balance, I want to know how many people feel strongly enough about this to join me, sign my petition, and send a message to the White House and Congressional Republicans.

name: Marina Jenkins, Team AOTL
subject: Anti-democracy leaders are trying to undermine the power of the vote
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
Our work is nowhere near done, and we need your help.
|
---

Peter,
I’ll be frank with you: Our work to fight for fair maps has made historic progress, though it’s nowhere near complete.
Looking around the country, it’s clear that far too many states remain heavily gerrymandered — including Texas, North Carolina, and Wisconsin. Unlike the times when states redrew maps once a decade, we’re living in an era of perpetual redistricting, with anti-democracy leaders trying to undermine voters’ power and distort maps for partisan gain.
**Take a look at what just happened in Alabama.** A federal court ruled that Black Alabamians will continue to vote under a fair, representative congressional map. _But this was no easy fight, and did not happen overnight. _
This followed **YEARS** of relentless efforts by anti-democracy lawmakers to dilute the power of Black voters, despite them making up 27% of the voting age population. After Alabama lawmakers enacted a congressional map that allowed Black Alabamians the opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice in _only one district_ , **our affiliate, the National Redistricting Foundation, challenged the map,** which resulted in**the US Supreme Court striking the map down for violating Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) in _Allen v. Milligan._ That victory led directly to today’s fairer, more representative map.**
[**Our team at All On The Line will always fight back against anti-democracy forces wherever they try to undermine voters’ power. Can you make a contribution today to help fight gerrymandering and fund this grassroots work?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108036955/546650806/-766669670?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_Fight4Maps_email_20250520-AOTL-Tier1-Fight4Maps-MJ_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250520-AOTL-Tier1-Fight4Maps-MJ_&utm_campaign=Fight4Maps&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJhMTZiMDdmLTkzMzUtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=A0DX3OLsLS7bUSmh5Mm6z-z7ZoGxKu-X0lmVla9BAss=)

Right now, there’s another Supreme Court case under consideration out of Louisiana that we all need to pay close attention to.
Here are the facts:
→ In 2022, the National Redistricting Foundation and other partners brought a case in the state of Louisiana **seeking two Black opportunity congressional districts** where Black voters could elect the candidates of their choice — a right guaranteed by the Voting Rights Act and affirmed in the court’s _Allen v. Milligan_ ruling.
→ At that time, **only one of six** districts was designed this way — despite the fact that Black residents make up nearly one-third of Louisiana’s population.
→ After the map was redrawn to be in compliance with the Voting Rights Act, inclusive of the two Black opportunity districts, **a new lawsuit was filed** by a group of self-proclaimed “non-African American voters” claiming that the updated map takes race into consideration too heavily.
In the weeks ahead, the Supreme Court will decide whether the new map, drawn specifically to comply with the Voting Rights Act, will remain. The outcome of this case could _**fundamentally**_ reshape how Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act is applied across the country.
**If the Court rules against the fair map, it could set a dangerous precedent that severely limits voters’ power — specifically the voting power of Black people and other communities of color to challenge map manipulation. **
[**All On The Line is actively fighting to defend fair maps, but we can’t do it alone. Make a contribution today, and let’s do everything we can to fight for our democracy.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108037014/546650950/-766669670?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_Fight4Maps_email_20250520-AOTL-Tier1-Fight4Maps-MJ_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250520-AOTL-Tier1-Fight4Maps-MJ_&utm_campaign=Fight4Maps&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJhMTZiMDdmLTkzMzUtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=A0DX3OLsLS7bUSmh5Mm6z-z7ZoGxKu-X0lmVla9BAss=)

clean_body: Our work is nowhere near done, and we need your help. | Peter, I’ll be frank with you: Our work to fight for fair maps has made historic progress, though it’s nowhere near complete. Looking around the country, it’s clear that far too many states remain heavily gerrymandered — including Texas, North Carolina, and Wisconsin. Unlike the times when states redrew maps once a decade, we’re living in an era of perpetual redistricting, with anti-democracy leaders trying to undermine voters’ power and distort maps for partisan gain. Take a look at what just happened in Alabama. A federal court ruled that Black Alabamians will continue to vote under a fair, representative congressional map. But this was no easy fight, and did not happen overnight. This followed YEARS of relentless efforts by anti-democracy lawmakers to dilute the power of Black voters, despite them making up 27% of the voting age population. After Alabama lawmakers enacted a congressional map that allowed Black Alabamians the opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice in only one district , our affiliate, the National Redistricting Foundation, challenged the map, which resulted inthe US Supreme Court striking the map down for violating Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) in Allen v. Milligan. That victory led directly to today’s fairer, more representative map. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Right now, there’s another Supreme Court case under consideration out of Louisiana that we all need to pay close attention to. Here are the facts: → In 2022, the National Redistricting Foundation and other partners brought a case in the state of Louisiana seeking two Black opportunity congressional districts where Black voters could elect the candidates of their choice — a right guaranteed by the Voting Rights Act and affirmed in the court’s Allen v. Milligan ruling. → At that time, only one of six districts was designed this way — despite the fact that Black residents make up nearly one-third of Louisiana’s population. → After the map was redrawn to be in compliance with the Voting Rights Act, inclusive of the two Black opportunity districts, a new lawsuit was filed by a group of self-proclaimed “non-African American voters” claiming that the updated map takes race into consideration too heavily. In the weeks ahead, the Supreme Court will decide whether the new map, drawn specifically to comply with the Voting Rights Act, will remain. The outcome of this case could fundamentally reshape how Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act is applied across the country. If the Court rules against the fair map, it could set a dangerous precedent that severely limits voters’ power — specifically the voting power of Black people and other communities of color to challenge map manipulation. = = Thanks again for your support — more from me soon. Marina Jenkins Executive Director, All On The Line is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. name: Ashley from Team Taylor
subject: We beat Elon Musk before. Let’s do it again.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
Elon Musk spent over $20 million trying to buy a seat on our state’s highest court. If he tries to do it again, we need to be ready.
#

Peter,
In the last Wisconsin Supreme Court race, Elon Musk poured over $20 million into our state, trying to install a right-wing extremist who’d rubber-stamp their assault on our rights and freedoms.
And Elon Musk failed.
People across Wisconsin stood up, fought back, and won. We protected our progressive majority and sent a message that our democracy is _not_ for sale.
**But now, Elon Musk could be coming back with a vengeance.**
Judge Chris Taylor — an attorney, lifelong advocate, and now a judge who’s spent her career fighting for fairness and freedom — is running to unseat Justice Rebecca Bradley, a far-right voice on the court who thrives on division and ideology.
Bradley’s track record caters to special interests, not Wisconsinites. **If right-wing billionaires like Elon flood our state with cash again, we can’t afford to be caught flat-footed.**
This grassroots team beat them once, and we can do it again, but only if we start building our campaign war chest now.
[**Will you become a founding donor by pitching in $10 to Chris Taylor’s campaign and Ruben Gallego today?**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-taylor-split.VUfLWJ&refcode=em250520-1-90&t=1&akid=3872%2E734936%2E7w4Dhf)

name: Lindsey Graham
subject: No silver spoon
body:
Fellow American, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. |
---
Fellow American,

I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina.

I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses.

[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nY-Y16ciw9mPdnUZ4S75PFnB_qrfcAnUCJM_-qWnRmlzwjFoShCaYPJld3USbyeqeNQ-f-7t5x1Tv8jLijsjulMt7O3kCQuS6Zb8Z0cFNd5h3a6Er3ug1vaDn_OJoRzcoFEN2KVbu4E0wCfECaZC4TlHh62s1SqGDa70OKGowkXmL7rk1yxQBsdJkKiHXY18CZPTg9jTYUP_MxxdzWZSWszOXh5hEXhVAnnp80EVYLbcZvZdDvc5VqfKbOLk9jBnsesEPoREjzpicA_onoNq4F9xMK0czbhkTNWrpwzf6E_y0gku4NcCVD5yJ-DV56XKFMYHu7e1ttRSisfAKO0nyYqMSZf_fJ1-92TNVKi-3Og5lzoYDDYajebRqGjQC0kp_Q%3D%3D)
**I had to grow up fast, Fellow American,_and I learned many life lessons at an early age._**

After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney.

Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq.

[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nY-Y16ciw9mPdnUZ4S75PFnB_qrfcAnUCJM_-qWnRmlzwjFoShCaYPJld3USbyeqeNQ-f-7t5x1Tv8jLijsjulMt7O3kCQuS6Zb8Z0cFNd5h3a6Er3ug1vaDn_OJoRzcoFEN2KVbu4E0wCfECaZC4TlHh62s1SqGDa70OKGowkXmL7rk1yxQBsdJkKiHXY18CZPTg9jTYUP_MxxdzWZSWszOXh5hEXhVAnnp80EVYLbcJYDnBBL-F2QZdhAK5h0lnAEWfq5cOgTRBQYr3Fbbfq8W95fgkd8rI4ilDYbm2PRNluR6Vac-MXTh8S2DWCO0-EZBjHw6ffvrVPRJxP9wwOYE_SLT5p4sQJrgWR7hNjNF1JN0qouag8CajpqVKP3gJA%3D%3D)
Fellow American, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility.

But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need.

**It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on.**

**We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from.**

Fellow American, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families.

**[That's why I'm reaching out with a simple request: if you have a few dollars to spare, would you consider making a contribution to support my campaign by clicking this link or the button below?](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nY-Y16ciw9mPdnUZ4S75PFnB_qrfcAnUCJM_-qWnRmlzwjFoShCaYPJld3USbyeqeNQ-f-7t5x1Tv8jLijsjulMt7O3kCQuS6Zb8Z0cFNd5h3a6Er3ug1vaDn_OJoRzcoFEN2KVbu4E0wCfECaZC4TlHh62s1SqGDa70OKGowkXmL7rk1yxQBsdJkKiHXY18CZPTg9jTYUP_MxxdzWZSWszOXh5hEXhVAnnp80EVYLbcrIigP_2JjlbP-kkmvxdqjCaXWaxVt6hxv9XuFzlfz9bPmeLp35pYjINKcfDWBuFtzsVkI_GXPF40BQ6hpxImy2Um7So8iBIeRRB79h1nf4ryPXgL_nzumFcqQLMw5Y9oWJ85TACw_OWL2B-8-BSIeQ%3D%3D)**

| [** CONTRIBUTE HERE **](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nY-Y16ciw9mPdnUZ4S75PFnB_qrfcAnUCJM_-qWnRmlzwjFoShCaYPJld3USbyeqeNQ-f-7t5x1Tv8jLijsjulMt7O3kCQuS6Zb8Z0cFNd5h3a6Er3ug1vaDn_OJoRzcoFEN2KVbu4E0wCfECaZC4TlHh62s1SqGDa70OKGowkXmL7rk1yxQBsdJkKiHXY18CZPTg9jTYUP_MxxdzWZSWszOXh5hEXhVAnnp80EVYLbcLRlakugM_3CjHCjyLAF11uMCZ_mWTvsdJtqlg8zkegc89UxKO0lUhu4Fkugvc9CU2zs89-D2wjRUyQAMBUGVx0yZbVqelcn2r6bY3elq3mV-N5P9JVU3Siu_QzyIu07WssmbQY2QAqBjeLS-PLJx2A%3D%3D)
---

If you're unable to contribute financially, I understand. I'd greatly appreciate it if you could forward this email along to a friend or family member to help amplify my message.

Together, I know we can build the country that protects our communities, rewards hard work, and ensures every hard-working American has a shot at their dreams, just like I was so fortunate to have.

Thank you for taking a moment out of your busy day to read this message.

Sincerely,
Lindsey Graham

[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nbDVKyMBjn7B_jYdsYXYh75xK3YFjZK1PcyB1tnWopTIslOyIG9QSPAusu0-lvqjHB4f5LRYI-fD386Jn5CWmRaw-pWScsvg7IpismlkANY2ewmaonphkzfe4y-uRhpj91B4v-AtRRYq-DK-eLQU9AOK4KkXdoM7yNx5qhnNX6biZiVgQ1oejWmOz_LAh1RalH0ENLBuqcJwF91wUfec49E%3D)

[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=44ababad8abb4094a4ff53409af5d065&_e=3gZBUKtWvNci-2bdGYo8nY-Y16ciw9mPdnUZ4S75PFnB_qrfcAnUCJM_-qWnRmlzwjFoShCaYPJld3USbyeqeNQ-f-7t5x1Tv8jLijsjulMt7O3kCQuS6Zb8Z0cFNd5h3a6Er3ug1vaDn_OJoRzcoFEN2KVbu4E0wCfECaZC4TlHh62s1SqGDa70OKGowkXmL7rk1yxQBsdJkKiHXY18CZPTg9jTYUP_MxxdzWZSWszOXh5hEXhVAnnp80EVYLbcmJYqbqGaoKN2DhSRYpM_nam-eVt9kaGIAZvDd4dEZXO0VT44_tx8_U_VPzMj7nNNhWmyrZu75s806aDvekqvsNJF1hbrlx77Fmii42-OaeqDFZN11hY80Uhmfn446X5DX-3Wuot9F9ReE1spOtnuaA%3D%3D)
In the United States Senate, South Carolina's Lindsey Graham has earned a reputation as a conservative, a problem-solver, and a leader who gets things done. He is a great friend to our men and women serving in uniform, a respected voice on national security matters, a leading advocate for cutting the out-of-control spending in Washington, and a champion for confirming conservative judges.

clean_body: Fellow American, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. | Fellow American, I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses. I had to grow up fast, Fellow American,and I learned many life lessons at an early age. After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney. Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq. Fellow American, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility. But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need. It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on. We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from. Fellow American, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families. | If you're unable to contribute financially, I understand. I'd greatly appreciate it if you could forward this email along to a friend or family member to help amplify my message. Together, I know we can build the country that protects our communities, rewards hard work, and ensures every hard-working American has a shot at their dreams, just like I was so fortunate to have. Thank you for taking a moment out of your busy day to read this message. Sincerely, Lindsey Graham In the United States Senate, South Carolina's Lindsey Graham has earned a reputation as a conservative, a problem-solver, and a leader who gets things done. He is a great friend to our men and women serving in uniform, a respected voice on national security matters, a leading advocate for cutting the out-of-control spending in Washington, and a champion for confirming conservative judges. | Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Air Force or the Department of Defense. body:
Here’s what your support really powers.

We know you probably get a lot of email messages begging for money, often from sources that are unclear about their intentions. We bet you’ve seen those emails that shout things like _“600% Match Ends Tonight!!!”_ or make absurd claims that “missing a donation” will doom the entire movement forever. **That’s not how we do things here at JanePAC. You’re not an ATM to us. We see you as a partner in this movement — someone who deserves honesty and a clear understanding of where your support goes.** When you chip in to our movement, here’s what you’re investing in:
* Your support helps us **elect climate champions in critical down-ballot races** — from state legislatures to city councils — where some of the most important decisions around pollution, clean energy, and public health are made every day.

* Your support helps us **recruit volunteers for endorsed candidates, rally voters to the polls, and run digital campaigns on social media** that reach people who’ve never heard of these races — but will feel their impact.

* Your support helps us **provide campaign strategies and build grassroots infrastructure around leaders** who are ready to take on Big Oil and WIN.

We would be lying if we said we didn’t have any deadlines or track our fundraising progress — we do. But we’ll never guilt-trip you or scare you into donating, because we believe in treating our community with respect. If you choose to donate, it’s because you believe in this work. And that means _everything_ to us. **[Would you consider making a donation to help us power this movement forward? We just announced our Virginia Climate Slate — so every cent you give today will help us defend the Virginia House, fight for climate action, and elect climate champions through 2025 and beyond.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108039467/546658322/2105508926?refcode=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImEzMjk1OWIyLTk2MzUtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Zr8K56QU2AWTSacqprgGfUFAmD4ZCsvHulqA2drw5_Q=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108039468/546658323/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImEzMjk1OWIyLTk2MzUtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Zr8K56QU2AWTSacqprgGfUFAmD4ZCsvHulqA2drw5_Q=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108039469/546658324/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.a32959b2-9635-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImEzMjk1OWIyLTk2MzUtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Zr8K56QU2AWTSacqprgGfUFAmD4ZCsvHulqA2drw5_Q=)

clean_body: Here’s what your support really powers. | We know you probably get a lot of email messages begging for money, often from sources that are unclear about their intentions. We bet you’ve seen those emails that shout things like “600% Match Ends Tonight!!!” or make absurd claims that “missing a donation” will doom the entire movement forever. That’s not how we do things here at JanePAC. You’re not an ATM to us. We see you as a partner in this movement — someone who deserves honesty and a clear understanding of where your support goes. When you chip in to our movement, here’s what you’re investing in: Your support helps us elect climate champions in critical down-ballot races — from state legislatures to city councils — where some of the most important decisions around pollution, clean energy, and public health are made every day. Your support helps us recruit volunteers for endorsed candidates, rally voters to the polls, and run digital campaigns on social media that reach people who’ve never heard of these races — but will feel their impact. Your support helps us provide campaign strategies and build grassroots infrastructure around leaders who are ready to take on Big Oil and WIN. We would be lying if we said we didn’t have any deadlines or track our fundraising progress — we do. But we’ll never guilt-trip you or scare you into donating, because we believe in treating our community with respect. If you choose to donate, it’s because you believe in this work. And that means everything to us. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks for everything you do. – The team at JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

## This week on The Sean Spicer Show...
Tuesday, Dan Turrentine will do a deep dive into the Democrat bench, and Josh Hammer joining me Wednesday. Thursday I'll be breaking down each of the deptartments' accomplishments. Finally, Friday we'll revisit my conversation with Alex Bruesewitz.
## Trump takes on big pharma...
Trump's been piling up wins left and right.
He's taken big steps to stabilize the Middle East, he's brokering peace to end the conflict in Ukraine, and he's bringing common sense back to American foreign policy.
But I want to talk about one of Trump's domestic wins.
He's taking on big pharma-- and I think it's not a moment too soon.
For too long, politicians have been all talk when it comes to drug prices. But true to character, Trump is actually delivering on his promise to put America first.
Last week, he announced an executive order lowering drug prices and leveling the international playing field.
Here's the [order](https://substack.com/redirect/9b9e50ca-0167-4c94-ab53-a14f5bd072ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for reference.
This action is huge for ending the price-gouging and double-dealing that's been plaguing America for too long.
So how did Trump do it?...

Stories I'm reading... \- via CBS News \- via The Washington Post \- via The Daily Wire \- via Washington Examiner \- via The Federalist \- via Vatican News \- via Fox News This week on The Sean Spicer Show... Tuesday, Dan Turrentine will do a deep dive into the Democrat bench, and Josh Hammer joining me Wednesday. Thursday I'll be breaking down each of the deptartments' accomplishments. Finally, Friday we'll revisit my conversation with Alex Bruesewitz. Trump takes on big pharma... Trump's been piling up wins left and right. He's taken big steps to stabilize the Middle East, he's brokering peace to end the conflict in Ukraine, and he's bringing common sense back to American foreign policy. But I want to talk about one of Trump's domestic wins. He's taking on big pharma-- and I think it's not a moment too soon. For too long, politicians have been all talk when it comes to drug prices. But true to character, Trump is actually delivering on his promise to put America first. Last week, he announced an executive order lowering drug prices and leveling the international playing field. Here's the for reference. This action is huge for ending the price-gouging and double-dealing that's been plaguing America for too long. So how did Trump do it?...
