Friend, we came up short last year. [_**19,006 votes or 0.34% short to be exact.**_](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR-Gz-C6P_z6WyDD52djUYn8qcm9Qq5OpA5xWhc2RC5Zk6X_NBTOAPK1y6r6MGeqpMfrEvGhpxWdXPWwBSmzLga4ODyU6D316dwxsKyWQH0iGreA0DaME8KN93GB4XCcAWcj7pTm6SmN1gN98RPSzAzI%3D) [
**But I’m back running for Senate out of the great state of Michigan once and again, and I can assure you that we will not have a repeat of 2024.**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR9BLdOtI09FuRqJAGxPqvdD7iXBMGxVzlcNjSdEn1Mu_F-3FeSYos4tlDo5Di1xkPLbJ4XdSlhHlME61lAvsfSy-luaPygTtu9OKuaX1evb91VszuNQDO6eJNLHzSLCkrltKSZ0rR0pdz-yOPyiT71Y%3D)

| | [ **FLIP MICHIGAN RED** ](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR47KtNbv_uy4K_II9f1xvHuy8cfShow4UwY2L_qqr2GK7N0olIodsXE9Az3ke6j9AN4kxg9hRQGuROvU3o5lQADLwb2abzHHu4zlWpYC_UXdtjwOK4eGwBEwAFFf7gM11JF56-SrM2FwR4UzUCzZhtI%3D)
---
_Let me share a few reasons why this time will be different:_

**FIRST** , during my first run for Senate, I was a longtime Congressman with little to no name ID. This cycle, I will be entering the race as an established candidate who earned the support of nearly 2.7 million Michiganders.

**SECOND** , President Trump's victory in my state was a watershed moment. Michigan was long seen as a key pillar to the “blue wall” – in other words, a state that consistently votes Democratic. We’re entering the midterms with an entirely different landscape.

**THIRD** , the Democrats are already running with their tail between their legs. Gary Peters, our incumbent Senator, decided he would rather quit his job than face me in a race he was sure he would lose.
---
[ **HELP MIKE WIN** ](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR__Ee2KfYar0B3mtIdYst-F1MiXgrLHgh0Me48eomNj4MLT1YUIbdqrLiRH3Ngs7uOGLNoSTIO1An9oFy2tYxnY7nmawNgBH9iKtEhv24DV6MftvbQvn_XjsqnbXEmh0mbsRjgCT5rIEnnzz7moAqyE%3D)
---
| | This open race is a Republican’s best chance to flip a Michigan seat since 1994, Friend!

If I’m going to win, I will need a top-notch team by my side and it would mean the world if you would join me. I’m ready to get to work on day one and be a man you will be proud to call Michigan's next Senator.

_**[To help prepare me for victory, I’m calling on every patriot reading this email to rush in my average donation of $17.50 or more right now. Will you step up and answer the call?](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR6odNxBjXo3CuGbvSL7MIpzrI_cbMPter4mzHkiK3ULzKjqiIJpiYFVsmtW4giGUCJ8LDZtn_hrGH1gCUJWfPvHWbIy3oqJQoX6PLE3pJoQTmvoujt_4dhGtFUjiy1yx9bGYZFvQ1o6QDziUGoy6Axo%3D)**_

[ **EXPAND TRUMP'S SENATE MAJORITY** ](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=b2cd4f664bfb4662985f2d17f521157a&_e=sKAnjVVlpxbzYqMGJ3qVUlILCtafugVF5Lz6TMLu3jMwpdO-N25kXOeCOsu-lBF0kmHfLX8kZ95xJpQmnUbHPBkZnwLlBuGivyqTXBiDg5OWpjVG653Lv9uVZ_9C2tK95parkT4ZH4h57tykiKwHDmmFgEO317ETi62yhX71JlKDsDvb3D9vwe_D74piibm-aB70wVTb79cdOeYl4aSB7nFRUPzUA9OpjDQn00AvWqnZQ1Dty4d5QBCHNV8y7goRNHzQ4TIpVg1mRmqQbmPusjz8kxeDrhO-0XGj3NcwtvmqYKUGP_LgP8QcdLdmiXmQYA7pyKmHE6gKRBOHgIZIR9brkxolHvOmrtZnZAxJ-DaW_Cawz-7E6P5PRtt8sErXe6uWUHc4QK7DkMG5FPxEmFuUvCfP9o1YzvW5S5mup-6jPKSHHslgodBaTXz1AuSYvSZg5xw5xFKosQZ5nH0mFcJhmCpUqIhEdNMtEIMBMLs%3D)

Thank you for reading,

Mike Rogers

# [The Keynote Speech I Wanted to Give, But Didn't](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164017837&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MDE3ODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc3NTkzNTQsImV4cCI6MTc1MDM1MTM1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.74bQ8WppuA_yWE4ewB_Um5xXKX3rF8a9-Lo1HpqljSU)
### A message to the next generation: I'm sorry. You deserve better.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| May 20
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164017837&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MDE3ODM3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0Nzc1OTM1NCwiZXhwIjoxNzUwMzUxMzU0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yjTjKnWQ_9kNC018BqdYm10OYEsBDX16t3oPQm8JVFw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164017837&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MDE3ODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc3NTkzNTQsImV4cCI6MTc1MDM1MTM1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.74bQ8WppuA_yWE4ewB_Um5xXKX3rF8a9-Lo1HpqljSU&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164017837&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0MDE3ODM3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc3NTkzNTQsImV4cCI6MTc1MDM1MTM1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.74bQ8WppuA_yWE4ewB_Um5xXKX3rF8a9-Lo1HpqljSU)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvdGhlLWtleW5vdGUtc3BlZWNoLWktd2FudGVkLXRvLWdpdmU_.3nyao8ZrNo7ktZyJWp4ZyF8H0POkiPiNc8_PI5KMNcw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/the-keynote-speech-i-wanted-to-give?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This week, I had the honor of delivering not one, but _two_ Army ROTC commencement addresses--first at Temple University in Philadelphia, then at the University of Rhode Island on Monday.
These weren't your typical graduations. Twenty-nine young men and women, after years of waking at 5 a.m. for physical training, studying leadership and tactics, and balancing the demands of college life, stood tall in uniform, raised their right hands, and became officers in the United States Army.
They weren't just earning degrees; they were earning their place in one of the most trusted professions in America: the U.S. military.
Becoming an Army officer in the United States is no small feat. Fewer than 7,000 Americans are commissioned each year: roughly 1,000 from West Point, 900 from Officer Candidate School, and around 5,000 through ROTC. More than 60,000 students start the process. Roughly 1 in 10 make it to the finish line.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/35c16630-aa93-44cf-b900-ac5fabdf223b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A few of the amazing Temple University students who were commissioned over the weekend.
That means fewer than one in ten earn a commission--and with it, the special trust and confidence of the President of the United States to lead America's sons and daughters in uniform.
I stood before them, offering the customary wisdom about leadership, sacrifice, and living a purpose-driven life.
I said what military speakers usually say: that leadership is a sacred responsibility. That military service is a form of moral commitment--of doing something bigger than yourself. That they now carry the "special trust and confidence" of the President of the United States. I quoted Patton, told war stories, and tried to offer the kind of wisdom you hope sticks with someone at the start of a lifelong journey.
Thanks for reading, subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wYXRyaWNrbXVycGh5cGEuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNjQwMTc4MzcsInMiOjQzOTkxMzMsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDc3NTkzNTQsImV4cCI6MTc1MDM1MTM1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.gmLMa5xe6hb3vkFQQWbP57INI8rZ5W1_lrJSTqCSWKY?)
**Note: Since my address was to the future military leaders, and the military is an apolitical organization legally barred from political activity under the Hatch Act, I couldn 't talk about specific policies that are hurting our national security.**
But what I really wanted to say was this:
**I 'm sorry.**
#### **I 'm sorry for the debt**
My generation--and the one before mine--has left this country with a $36 trillion national debt. Nearly $900 billion a year now goes just to cover the interest. That's more than we spend on defense, education, or infrastructure. When the debt was $13.5 trillion in 2010 (and we still had troops in Iraq and Afghanistan), the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Admiral Mike Mullen said our national debt is "the greatest threat to our national security". The "greatest threat" is now about to be more than three times worse.
The current budget being debated in Congress would add another $4.5 trillion. Bloomberg has called it a fiscal disaster. The nonpartisan Penn Wharton Budget Model cites tax provisions approved by the House Ways and Means Committee increasing primary deficits by [$4,561 billion over 10 years.](https://substack.com/redirect/5dd41bbe-dc80-4741-93ae-b2d2218c28dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Neither party seems serious about solving the problem--and I get it, it's hard to see the forest for the trees. But the American people can still sense that something is deeply wrong. Only two presidents in modern history have left office with a balanced budget: Republican Dwight Eisenhower, a former Army general, and Bill Clinton, a Democrat from Arkansas. That's it.
We have no right to ask young people to serve and sacrifice while saddling them with the fiscal time bomb we're creating. This generation is literally borrowing money from the younger generation to give themselves a tax break - and the tax break spend the Wharton Budget Model shows how "The top 10% of the income distribution receives about 65 percent of the total value of the legislation."
### **I 'm sorry for our broken healthcare system**
We spend almost 20% of our GDP on healthcare--$4.9 trillion a year-- yet we rank behind every peer country in life expectancy and health outcomes. Seventy percent of Americans are overweight or obese. One in five suffers from a mental health disorder, where most don't get treatment. And those who do often receive care that isn't grounded in best evidence-based practices.
We don't have a healthcare system. We have a sick-care system--one that waits for people to fall apart before it steps in.
**I 'm sorry for how we've failed Gen Z**
It's become fashionable to mock Gen Z as lazy, screen-addicted, and soft. That's unfair--and it's wrong.
Only 24% of Americans today are eligible to join the military. The leading disqualifiers are obesity and mental health. But the problem isn't a lack of willingness to serve--it's the system we've built. You can apply for a fast-food job today and start tomorrow. Want to serve your country? Get ready for months of red tape.
When I helped lead the Army, we studied Gen Z. Over 70% said they wanted to serve their communities or their country. That's more than the 56% who said the same during the Vietnam generation. And yet, we make it harder than ever for them to do so.
We also forgot the most important lesson of Vietnam: separate the war from the warrior. Back then, we didn't. We spit on soldiers, called them "baby killers," and turned their service into political theater. We should never repeat that mistake.
We're failing young people by not giving them an opportunity to serve their country: in the military, Peace Corps, Teach for America or other opportunities to do something greater than themselves.
And we've forgotten too many veterans who continue to come home from our generation's wars without political leadership that tackles the challenges they face. Fewer are becoming entrepreneurs and many earn less in the civilian workforce than they did on active duty. Veterans are also almost twice as likely to die by suicide.
### **I 'm Sorry for the Hypocrisy**
We tell Gen Z they're lazy and screen-addicted--but we're too often the ones who handed them tablets at age 3 so they'd stay quiet at dinner. We raised them on YouTube and iPads and then act surprised when they struggle to unplug.
They didn't invent the Internet. We gave it to them--and then blamed them for using it.
It's far easier to criticize a generation than to examine your own.
### **I remember the names**
Each Memorial Day, I visit a quiet corner of 24th and Aspen in Philadelphia. There, a small marble marker honors Patrick Ward--my mother's best friend and the man I was named after. He died in Vietnam saving his fellow soldiers and was awarded the Silver Star.
He was 19.
Most people walk by the marker without noticing. I never do. My father served them. So did my uncles in Vietnam.
I think of Patrick. I think of the 58,000 Americans who died in Vietnam. I think of the 19 soldiers I lost as a paratrooper in Iraq. I think of what we ask of these new officers--and how seldom our political class, in both parties, matches their courage.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5ece81ba-79b8-4b25-a613-a06686934d75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Visiting the Patrick Ward Memorial in Philadelphia
**On Building America**
After World War II, veterans came home and built the middle class. Half of them started small businesses--building iconic global brands like Walmart, Nike, and Comcast. Four out of every five members of Congress had military experience. Today, it's less than one in five. Now, less than 5% of my generation of Iraq and Afghanistan veterans start their own business.
At a time when [trust in government is at record lows,](https://substack.com/redirect/da9e5506-34e4-4a26-9f0d-46f4ed1b7953?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) when young people feel hopeless about politics and uncertain about the future, we need to ask: Are we giving them a country worth believing in?
A 2025 Harvard Kennedy School poll found that only 19% of individuals aged 18 to 29 trust the federal government to act in their best interests regularly. This erosion of trust reflects broader concerns about political polarization and institutional effectiveness.
## **[Harvard Youth Poll- Spring 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/da9e5506-34e4-4a26-9f0d-46f4ed1b7953?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): Key Findings**
### **Financial Struggles**
* Over 4 in 10 young Americans under 30 are "barely getting by" financially.
* Only 16% report doing well or very well financially.
* Financial hardship is more prevalent among women (47%), Hispanics (52%), and those without a college degree (50%).

### **Sense of Community**
* Only 17% of young Americans feel deeply connected to a community.

* Nearly 1 in 5 (18%) report no strong sense of belonging.

### **Trust in Institutions**
* Just 15% believe the country is heading in the right direction.
* Approval ratings: President Trump (31%), Congressional Republicans (29%), Congressional Democrats (23%).
* Only 19% trust the federal government to do the right thing most or all of the time.

### **I worry we aren 't giving them something to believe in**
I had lunch recently with a second-year law student in Miami. He voted last fall--but not for either major candidate for President. "I just didn't trust either," he told me. And he's not alone. Trust in government is at record lows. Civic engagement is eroding. Young people are disillusioned.
And why wouldn't they be? The political class talks in circles while the debt climbs. Wars rage. And the country feels less united by the year.
Congress is more divided than ever. The past three sessions have been among the least productive in our history. We bring in $5 trillion in revenue. We spend $7 trillion. And then we act surprised when voters lose faith.
Trust in government is collapsing. Young Americans are disillusioned--not because they're broken, but because the system is.
Are we giving them a country worth believing in?
### **What I Told Them Anyway**
At Temple, 6,000 students graduated. But only 14 were commissioned as Army officers. 15 were commissioned at University of Rhode Island. It's hard not to be inspired when you're around these young Great Americans. They'll leave college and enter a profession where the stakes are as high as they come--life and death, war and peace, leadership and loss.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9c51b1ac-200b-458c-80a8-e71f22ed6088?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I was honored to stand beside these great Americans at the University of Rhode Island.
As I sat in a Barnes & Noble drafting my remarks, earbuds in, I kept coming back to the poem _If_ by Rudyard Kipling--whose own son was killed in World War I:
> _" If you can fill the unforgiving minute
> With sixty seconds' worth of distance run,
> Yours is the Earth and everything that's in it."_
I also reminded them that failure is the tax of success. That grit matters. That leadership isn't about being perfect--it's about showing up when it counts.
### **What I hope they remember**
I hope they remember that I didn't offer just platitudes. I stood in front of them and told the truth. I stood there, looked them in the eyes, and said: _We have to do better!_
Sorry for the mess. Sorry for the burden. Sorry for the silence.
But I also made a promise: that I'm not giving up. That I will keep fighting for a government that earns their trust--and a country they can believe in.
And I asked my fellow Gen Xers to do the same.
Because this generation doesn't need lectures. They need leaders. And they deserve better than what we've given them.
-Patrick
Thanks for reading, subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

You deserve better. | | | May 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | This week, I had the honor of delivering not one, but two Army ROTC commencement addresses--first at Temple University in Philadelphia, then at the University of Rhode Island on Monday. These weren't your typical graduations. Twenty-nine young men and women, after years of waking at 5 a.m. for physical training, studying leadership and tactics, and balancing the demands of college life, stood tall in uniform, raised their right hands, and became officers in the United States Army. They weren't just earning degrees; they were earning their place in one of the most trusted professions in America: the U.S. military. Becoming an Army officer in the United States is no small feat. Fewer than 7,000 Americans are commissioned each year: roughly 1,000 from West Point, 900 from Officer Candidate School, and around 5,000 through ROTC. More than 60,000 students start the process. Roughly 1 in 10 make it to the finish line. | | ---|---|--- A few of the amazing Temple University students who were commissioned over the weekend. That means fewer than one in ten earn a commission--and with it, the special trust and confidence of the President of the United States to lead America's sons and daughters in uniform. I stood before them, offering the customary wisdom about leadership, sacrifice, and living a purpose-driven life. I said what military speakers usually say: that leadership is a sacred responsibility. That military service is a form of moral commitment--of doing something bigger than yourself. That they now carry the "special trust and confidence" of the President of the United States. I quoted Patton, told war stories, and tried to offer the kind of wisdom you hope sticks with someone at the start of a lifelong journey. Thanks for reading, subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? Note: Since my address was to the future military leaders, and the military is an apolitical organization legally barred from political activity under the Hatch Act, I couldn 't talk about specific policies that are hurting our national security. But what I really wanted to say was this: I 'm sorry. I 'm sorry for the debt My generation--and the one before mine--has left this country with a $36 trillion national debt. Nearly $900 billion a year now goes just to cover the interest. That's more than we spend on defense, education, or infrastructure. When the debt was $13.5 trillion in 2010 (and we still had troops in Iraq and Afghanistan), the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Admiral Mike Mullen said our national debt is "the greatest threat to our national security". The "greatest threat" is now about to be more than three times worse. The current budget being debated in Congress would add another $4.5 trillion. Bloomberg has called it a fiscal disaster. The nonpartisan Penn Wharton Budget Model cites tax provisions approved by the House Ways and Means Committee increasing primary deficits by Neither party seems serious about solving the problem--and I get it, it's hard to see the forest for the trees. But the American people can still sense that something is deeply wrong. Only two presidents in modern history have left office with a balanced budget: Republican Dwight Eisenhower, a former Army general, and Bill Clinton, a Democrat from Arkansas. That's it. We have no right to ask young people to serve and sacrifice while saddling them with the fiscal time bomb we're creating. name: LaMonica McIver
subject: BREAKING: These charges from the Trump administration are baseless

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver]
Friends,
The Trump administration just charged me with crimes for engaging in congressional oversight. This is a first—and it’s a flashing red light for our democracy.
I went to an ICE facility in my district to conduct oversight because that is my job and my lawful right as a member of Congress. It’s clear: Trump and his administration don’t want transparency or oversight, and they want to punish any person who dares to speak up.
These baseless, politically motivated charges against me make that even more obvious. We must stand up together against an administration that weaponizes law enforcement to persecute its opponents before it's too late. Democrats stand ready to fight back against the Trump administration’s overreach, and protect democracy and our Constitutional separation of powers.
We need your help. I’m asking you to split a donation between me and Sara Jacobs so we have the resources to win this battle for our democracy. We need 500 more grassroots donations before our deadline at midnight to make sure Republicans know we won’t be intimidated. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver]
This is far bigger than just me—the rule of law is at stake. Our democracy as we know it is under threat.
To ensure everyone who dares to speak truth to power is safe from political persecution, we must make as much noise and as big an impact as possible. Split a donation between me and Sara Jacobs and share this with your friends. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-mciver]
We're in this together,
LaMonica

| | [BREAKING: Trump admin issues criminal charges against Democratic Congresswoman](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108044195/546664554/266255970?amount=15&refcode=20250520_em_fr_b1_jeffries_main_v2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzM0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZjNjNzYyYy05YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z4X0MpvtIqko2P0PiRk6FNeVBFNdbapvMii3j0nNP8Q=) Peter, I need your immediate attention. Peter, It’s Congressman Eric Sorensen. I’m in my second term representing the greatest state in the world – Illinois. Today, I’m so happy to offer my endorsement for my good friend Kevin Morrison, who is running for Congress in Illinois’ Eighth Congressional District. Kevin is the grandson of immigrants, and a member of the LGBTQ+ community. As the first openly gay Congressman elected from Illinois, I know that we have to stick together, and I am proud to support Kevin. [**Peter, here’s a picture of the two of us when Kevin launched his campaign. Will you please chip in an $8 donation split between my campaign and Kevin Morrison’s to fuel our important work in Illinois?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108044373/546664820/31089275?refcode=em-5.20.25-all-a&amount=8&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1NDZiNy05YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ho5NbgSESJvjtRdalbUeOB8Ubr4DjOPKRWFCN3kodYk=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108044374/546664821/31089275?refcode=em-5.20.25-all-a&amount=8&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1NDZiNy05YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ho5NbgSESJvjtRdalbUeOB8Ubr4DjOPKRWFCN3kodYk=) This guy needs to come to Washington. He’s going to be the fighter for you. He understands already how to serve from his time as Cook County Commissioner, and I know he’s going to be the fighter that we need in Congress. Before I joined Congress, I was a meteorologist. It was my job to study weather patterns and make forecasts. And I’m forecasting Kevin Morrison in Congress – but only with the full strength of this grassroots team. [**I stepped up to support Kevin’s campaign, and now I need you to do the same. Will you please split a donation between my campaign and Kevin Morrison’s to help us build the resources that we need to win?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108044375/546664822/31089275?refcode=em-5.20.25-all-a&amount=8&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1NDZiNy05YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ho5NbgSESJvjtRdalbUeOB8Ubr4DjOPKRWFCN3kodYk=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108044377/546664823/31089275?refcode=em-5.20.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1NDZiNy05YjM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ho5NbgSESJvjtRdalbUeOB8Ubr4DjOPKRWFCN3kodYk=)

name: Yadira Caraveo
subject: why politics?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail]
Peter,
When I was working as a pediatrician, I often felt like my hands were tied. Countless families in our communities, families that I grew up with, were struggling. Prescription drug prices were out of control. Poor wages meant that families couldn’t afford treatments, and there were limits to what I could do as a doctor. So I ran for public office.
I ran to advocate for those in our community that have been left behind for too long, and I won.
As a Congresswoman I passed legislation lowering the costs of prescription drugs. I worked on a bipartisan bill to fight the fentanyl epidemic in our country. I made a difference in countless lives, but there’s a lot more work to do.
I’m running for Congress, and I can’t do it alone. Can I count on your support? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail]
Thank you,
Yadira
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ycfcemail]

name: Team GIFFORDS
subject: Not something we want to share
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team,
If you care about our movement for gun safety, please read this important message.
**As of this morning, more than 15,000 people have been killed or injured due to gun violence in 2025.**
Nearly 1 in 5 Americans say they have personally witnessed someone get shot. Gun violence is the #1 killer of children and teens in the country. Even more than car crashes and cancer.
But rather than addressing that, Donald Trump is canceling funds for hundreds of grants to local organizations working to end gun violence -- right before gun violence rates are expected to surge during the summer.
Your support helped us pass the first federal gun safety legislation in nearly 30 years. Now, we need your support again to protect all that we’ve accomplished together.
[**Will you please help us continue advancing our movement for gun safety before the summer begins? Our average donation is $27, but any amount you give will have a huge impact in the fight to make our communities -- and our country -- safer.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250520-1-b&t=1&akid=10451%2E3935704%2EeqEFx6)

name: New Dems
subject: Trump’s MAGA budget is moving fast, help us fight back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
Billionaire tax cuts. Cuts to Medicaid. Cuts to food stamps.

name: Lee Johnson
subject: I'm running to retire Lindsey Graham
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
I’m running because Lindsey Graham has forgotten about the people back home. He’s leading the charge to gut Social Security and Medicaid, and now, he backs the chaotic tariffs that are raising prices and cutting paychecks across South Carolina.
| [](https://fight4progress.org/johnson?t=tEUZOGDvqTDq&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-05-19_1&amount=25)
---
Peter,

**My name is Lee Johnson, and I’m running for the United States Senate to retire Lindsey Graham in South Carolina.**

I wasn’t born into politics—I was born in a construction family—operating heavy equipment before I could drive. My family taught me how to build things - like roads and bridges - and I’ve carried that with me everywhere: from construction sites and factory floors to boardrooms.

I’m running because Lindsey Graham has forgotten about the people back home. He’s leading the charge to gut Social Security and Medicaid, and now, he backs the chaotic tariffs that are raising prices and cutting paychecks across South Carolina.

We need honest leadership in Washington. I’ll fight to ban members of Congress from profiting off of public service. And I pledge to serve no more than two terms as your Senator, to ensure that our state has fresh, accountable leadership in Congress.

[_**This is a fight we can win, but we need your support. Can you split a donation between our campaign, and Fight For Progress today?**_](https://fight4progress.org/johnson?t=AumfXWgcz9Dj&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-05-19_1&amount=25)

I believe that Washington needs more builders - not dividers. That’s why I’m running for the U.S. Senate - to build an economy that works for everybody, not just the elite few.

Lindsey Graham hasn’t held a town hall in 8 years. He stopped listening, and we’re paying the price.

That’s why I’m hitting the road to listen to voters across South Carolina and make their voices heard. [_**If you can, split a donation between our campaign and Fight For Progress to help us reach voters across South Carolina and build a movement to beat the Washington establishment and fire Lindsey Graham.**_](https://fight4progress.org/johnson?t=CvpJrt5Nfe0B&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-05-19_1&amount=25)

From building infrastructure, to working on major water and energy projects, to leading major companies - I’ve spent my entire life building things from the ground up and fixing them when they break.

I know how tough it is just to get by right now. Prices are rising. Wages aren’t moving. The gap between the haves and have nots is widening. And politicians in Washington - like Lindsey Graham - have left us behind.

That’s why I’m running for the US Senate. [_**Split a donation between our campaign and Fight For Progress today to join our fight and together, we will fire Lindsey Graham.**_](https://fight4progress.org/johnson?t=UDAJHZkLRP3f&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-05-19_1&amount=25)

Thank you,

Lee
* * *
| | [

](https://fight4progress.org/wrapper?t=SgmImPdxjEtg&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-05-19_1&amount=25)
---

Washington, DC 20003

name: Mark Warner
subject: 📎 Sharing some old photos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047644/546670099/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) Derek,

I know I’ve been sending you a lot of emails about my reelection race in Virginia these days, but today, I want to take a step back and reintroduce myself.

[_**But first, I’m hoping you’ll consider chipping in to my reelection campaign to protect my seat and take back the Senate for Democrats.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047646/546670102/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=)

Like so many Americans, I grew up in a middle-class family and was lucky enough to attend good public schools. I have a vivid memory of being the Richard Nixon surrogate in my 8th grade class – yes I know, I’ve come a long way. I have my 8th grade history teacher, Jim Tyler, to thank for setting me on the path to being a Democrat. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047647/546670104/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) Fast forward a few years, I became the first person in my family to graduate from college, made possible by working multiple jobs and taking out student loans. That’s something I’m incredibly proud of.

Before I became a U.S. Senator, I actually started out in business. I invested my entire life savings, a few thousand dollars, into my first venture... which failed in about 6 weeks, by the way. My second venture did a little better, lasting six months, but it wasn’t until my third try that things started to look up. I’m extremely proud to have founded what became one of Virginia’s leading tech companies.

It was actually that experience that got me into public service. I thought that everybody in this country ought to have the same kind of fair shot that I had. So, I ran for governor in Virginia, and won. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047649/546670105/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) And if you can believe it, my good friend Tim Kaine (now my fellow Virginia senator) was my Lieutenant Governor. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047652/546670107/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) After our four years in the governor’s office, Virginia was rated the best state for business, the best managed state, _and_ the best state to receive a public education. But even then, I knew that my work to make sure that _every_ family – no matter their background – has the same shot that I did, was far from over.

That’s when I ran for the United States Senate, and won. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047653/546670109/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) In the Senate, I helped spearhead the largest infrastructure and public lands investments in half a century, worked to significantly expand Virginia’s domestic manufacturing, and have fought to lower health care costs across the Commonwealth. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108047656/546670111/-577349673?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Reintroduction_250520_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2YwMjdhZC05ZDM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iz_QOVjE5S0TPYhbMeqFBiAxl1JRPcK8oV6l3LH0WSc=) **But I have to tell you, in my time in the Senate – maybe in my time in politics – there has been nothing as challenging, as bizarre, and as _holy sugar_ as the last 130 days of the second Trump administration.**

This administration's corruption has made every American less safe. Trump is implementing tariffs causing prices to skyrocket for working families, refusing to fire Pete Hegseth even after he shared classified information on an unsecured platform, and backing the largest cuts to Medicaid in history to fund big tax giveaways for the wealthiest Americans.

I didn’t get into politics to let people like Trump and Elon dismantle our federal government and rebuild it for billionaires like themselves. I’m proud to lead the fight against their corrupt administration, but I can’t do it alone.

[_**So I’m asking: can I count on you to pitch in today to protect my seat in the Senate so I can keep up my work to hold this administration accountable and continue delivering for Virginia’s communities? clean_body: | | | = Derek, I know I’ve been sending you a lot of emails about my reelection race in Virginia these days, but today, I want to take a step back and reintroduce myself. = Like so many Americans, I grew up in a middle-class family and was lucky enough to attend good public schools. I have a vivid memory of being the Richard Nixon surrogate in my 8th grade class – yes I know, I’ve come a long way. I have my 8th grade history teacher, Jim Tyler, to thank for setting me on the path to being a Democrat. = Fast forward a few years, I became the first person in my family to graduate from college, made possible by working multiple jobs and taking out student loans. That’s something I’m incredibly proud of. Before I became a U.S. Senator, I actually started out in business. I invested my entire life savings, a few thousand dollars, into my first venture... which failed in about 6 weeks, by the way. My second venture did a little better, lasting six months, but it wasn’t until my third try that things started to look up. I’m extremely proud to have founded what became one of Virginia’s leading tech companies. It was actually that experience that got me into public service. I thought that everybody in this country ought to have the same kind of fair shot that I had. So, I ran for governor in Virginia, and won. = And if you can believe it, my good friend Tim Kaine (now my fellow Virginia senator) was my Lieutenant Governor. = After our four years in the governor’s office, Virginia was rated the best state for business, the best managed state, and the best state to receive a public education. But even then, I knew that my work to make sure that every family – no matter their background – has the same shot that I did, was far from over. That’s when I ran for the United States Senate, and won. = In the Senate, I helped spearhead the largest infrastructure and public lands investments in half a century, worked to significantly expand Virginia’s domestic manufacturing, and have fought to lower health care costs across the Commonwealth. = But I have to tell you, in my time in the Senate – maybe in my time in politics – there has been nothing as challenging, as bizarre, and as holy sugar as the last 130 days of the second Trump administration. This administration's corruption has made every American less safe. Trump is implementing tariffs causing prices to skyrocket for working families, refusing to fire Pete Hegseth even after he shared classified information on an unsecured platform, and backing the largest cuts to Medicaid in history to fund big tax giveaways for the wealthiest Americans. I didn’t get into politics to let people like Trump and Elon dismantle our federal government and rebuild it for billionaires like themselves. I’m proud to lead the fight against their corrupt administration, but I can’t do it alone. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = ******=**** ****=**** ****=**** ****=**** ****=**** The fight continues, Mark Warner ******=****** | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: This Senate seat is suddenly in play — are you in?
body:
Ken Paxton is on track to win the GOP primary — and that gives us a golden opportunity to FLIP this Senate seat.
But only if we act fast.
Paxton is corrupt, indicted, and completely tied to Trump’s toxic agenda. If he’s the nominee, this race becomes winnable — but only if we’re ready with the cash to compete.
We need 500 grassroots donors to chip in before midnight. That’s what it’ll take to build the war chest we need to go toe-to-toe with MAGA money. Rush $10 right now and help us seize this moment → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1]
No time to waste.
If we’re serious about flipping this Texas Senate seat, we need to start right now . The path is clear — and it runs straight through Ken Paxton.
🔵 Chip in $10 or more to help us seize this opening » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1]
Here’s where things stand:
Ken Paxton — the criminally indicted, Trump-worshipping MAGA extremist — is beating John Cornyn in the GOP primary. And let’s be honest: it’s not “if,” it’s when he becomes the nominee.
That hands us an opening we’ve never had before:
A scandal-plagued MAGA loyalist
Tied to Trump’s sinking ship
Running on corruption, chaos, and authoritarianism
This is a gift — but only if we’re ready to take advantage.
We can’t afford to wait. Republicans are already preparing to pour millions into this race. We need to arm our Democratic nominee with the momentum, message, and money to WIN.
Rush a donation right now to build our war chest before it’s too late. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052025_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052025_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052025_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052025_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052025_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1&amount=25]
Make no mistake: This isn’t just about Texas. This is about the future of the Senate. The future of our economy. The future of our democracy.
Trump and Paxton want to burn it all down. We can stop them — if we start fighting back today . [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052025_1]
Let’s go.
- The Real Texas PAC

name: LaMonica McIver
subject: I was charged by the Trump administration because I was doing my job
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
It’s clear: Trump and his administration don’t want transparency or oversight, and they want to punish any person who dares to speak up.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108048100/546670747/-523078770?refcode=email_052025_McIver&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAyYTUwYy05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VOfrdOrPck0W5S4pD62ZKpjUfsqpWggYEXVNNEEOip4=) Friend, The Trump administration just charged me with crimes for engaging in congressional oversight. This is a first—and it’s a flashing red light for our democracy. I went to an ICE facility in my district to conduct oversight because that is my job and my lawful right as a member of Congress. It’s clear: Trump and his administration don’t want transparency or oversight, and they want to punish any person who dares to speak up. These baseless, politically motivated charges against me make that even more obvious. We must stand up together against an administration that weaponizes law enforcement to persecute its opponents before it's too late. Democrats stand ready to fight back against the Trump administration’s overreach, and protect democracy and our Constitutional separation of powers. **[We need your help. I’m asking you to split a donation between me and Andrea Salinas so we have the resources to win this battle for our democracy. We need 500 more grassroots donations before our deadline at midnight to make sure Republicans know we won’t be intimidated.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108048101/546670748/-523078770?refcode=email_052025_McIver&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAyYTUwYy05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VOfrdOrPck0W5S4pD62ZKpjUfsqpWggYEXVNNEEOip4=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108048102/546670749/-523078770?express_lane=true&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=5&refcode=email_052025_McIver&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAyYTUwYy05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VOfrdOrPck0W5S4pD62ZKpjUfsqpWggYEXVNNEEOip4=)

This is far bigger than just me—the rule of law is at stake. Our democracy as we know it is under threat.
**[To ensure everyone who dares to speak truth to power is safe from political persecution, we must make as much noise and as big an impact as possible. body:
We need your input before this hits the Oval Office

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.) The Big, Beautiful Bill, backed by President Trump and House Republicans, includes: **✅ NO tax on TIPS** **✅ NO tax on OVERTIME** [](https://trk.emails.troydowning._Qo0CyTOP4LObL5fLtA) Peter,
President Trump promised to fight for working Americans. Now, he’s delivering.
He calls it “the largest tax cut in history,” which means more money in **YOUR** pocket and **LESS** for the D.C. Swamp.
This is America First leadership in action, but the Left and establishment RINOs are already gearing up to KILL this bill before it reaches the Oval Office to be signed into law.
Peter, if you support President Trump’s plan to cut taxes and reduce spending, we need your help forcing the Swamp’s hand.
[Do YOU support President Trump’s ‘Big, Beautiful Bill’?](https://trk.emails.troydowning._CiGNFBIL1v9Cg) [ CAST YOUR VOTE NOW
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-wCrro93F4yf79NRwcw) We need **500 patriots to step up in the next 24 hours** to help ensure this historic bill passes and moves us one step closer to making America great again.
Your support is crucial in securing victory for working Americans and standing up to the Washington elites.
Thank you, Peter, GOP Alerts - Team Downing | | |

name: Elect Women HQ
subject: Virginians are ready for change
body:
Setting the stage for major victories in 2025, 2026, and beyond |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108048300/546671426/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE549_20250520_SpanbergerDive_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056195&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzQyMTEzNC05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8QL3U2apxjHx0Ek2WXqjbHs5gBWiIpgKzNA7wbPLyM=) Peter,

In 2023, we flipped the Virginia House and held control of the Virginia Senate.

Now, in 2025, we're ready to flip the governor's seat and elect pro-choice champion **Abigail Spanberger** as the first woman governor in the state's history.

[**Want to be part of history? Donate now to help elect women like Abigail Spanberger and keep our pro-choice momentum going:**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108048312/546671439/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE549_20250520_SpanbergerDive_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056195&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzQyMTEzNC05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8QL3U2apxjHx0Ek2WXqjbHs5gBWiIpgKzNA7wbPLyM=) | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108048314/546671448/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE549_20250520_SpanbergerDive_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056195&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzQyMTEzNC05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8QL3U2apxjHx0Ek2WXqjbHs5gBWiIpgKzNA7wbPLyM=)
---
The 2023 elections signaled that Virginians are ready for pro-choice leadership at every level of government, and _The Cook Political Report_ just moved this race from "Toss-Up" to "Lean Democrat." This is one of our _best_ chances to flip a governor's seat this cycle, and the momentum is on our side.

**That's why we're proud to support Abigail Spanberger, the pro-choice champion running to stop her opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, and the Virginia GOP’s anti-choice agenda in its tracks.**

If Winsome Earle-Sears becomes governor, the GOP will see this as a green light and a mandate to continue with their extremist, undemocratic agenda — not just in Virginia, but _nationwide_.

But if Abigail Spanberger becomes governor this year, her victory would send a BIG signal that Democrats are ready to fight back and win BIG in 2026.

Let's prove that Democrats have what it takes to win in 2025, 2026, and beyond.

clean_body: Setting the stage for major victories in 2025, 2026, and beyond | = Peter, In 2023, we flipped the Virginia House and held control of the Virginia Senate. Now, in 2025, we're ready to flip the governor's seat and elect pro-choice champion Abigail Spanberger as the first woman governor in the state's history. = | = The 2023 elections signaled that Virginians are ready for pro-choice leadership at every level of government, and The Cook Political Report just moved this race from "Toss-Up" to "Lean Democrat." This is one of our best chances to flip a governor's seat this cycle, and the momentum is on our side. That's why we're proud to support Abigail Spanberger, the pro-choice champion running to stop her opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, and the Virginia GOP’s anti-choice agenda in its tracks. If Winsome Earle-Sears becomes governor, the GOP will see this as a green light and a mandate to continue with their extremist, undemocratic agenda — not just in Virginia, but nationwide. But if Abigail Spanberger becomes governor this year, her victory would send a BIG signal that Democrats are ready to fight back and win BIG in 2026. Let's prove that Democrats have what it takes to win in 2025, 2026, and beyond. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108048478/546672040/-729519538?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTUxYmFmMi05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=v0I7OKbNrGlXCR2NgTwW9TBaHgqNg4oRZ5eJgUuWQDI=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108048480/546672044/-1751156451?refcode=em-5.20.25-inactives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTUxYmFmMi05ZTM1LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=v0I7OKbNrGlXCR2NgTwW9TBaHgqNg4oRZ5eJgUuWQDI=) Hi there, it’s Marc Elias. I’m proud to be one of the leading lawyers in the ongoing fight to defend democracy and protect voting rights in the face of Republican extremism. Right now, we’re seeing Donald Trump and Elon Musk launch an assault on our government and the rule of law. They’re spreading dangerous lies, attacking our institutions, and working to undermine our democracy for _their_ own gain. **But here’s the truth: We’re not going to let them get away with it.** My friends at the Democratic Association of Secretaries of State are standing up to these ongoing threats, but they can’t do this alone. They need Democrats like you in this fight, friend. That’s why I’m asking you to make a donation to the DASS Legal Fund today. clean_body: Background on Kash Patel declaring an end to politicization of the FBI [NEWSLINK] BREAKING NEWS... [image] [] RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS IN PERIL "When it comes to fighting for the American people’s ‘right to know,’ no one holds a candle to Tom Fitton and his team at Judicial Watch" - SEAN HANNITY Tom Fitton returns with an exhaustive investigation into the progressive movement’s efforts to dismantle the venerable institutions of American rights and freedoms. Order Tom Fitton's Must-Read Today! [] [600x300] [] FBI's top boss Kash Patel says bureau ran cover for Hillary but it all ends under Trump [] Source: Fox News [] Patel pointed to the FBI's handling of the Hillary Clinton email investigation as an example of political bias and institutional failure. "The FBI hijacked the constitutional responsibility of the Department of Justice and the Attorney General, and James Comey and others specifically decided what cases to prosecute and not prosecute." [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] Background Notes from Judicial Watch [180x150x1] [] February 20, 2025 Kash Patel should expose the FBI's abuses of power [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton: "Kash Patel is the perfect choice to reform the FBI. Kash will bring transparency and accountability back to an agency that became irredeemably corrupt and the tip of the spear in unfettered lawfare against any American seen as a threat to the ruling class. name: Lindsey Graham
subject: Why I’m fighting for our future.
body:
Peter, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. |
---
Peter,

I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina.

I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses.

[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG65deU9ACd2HDdg2ggNkIUg5ua5X5cfMLJVVxfqyOMGFC1B2sshQso6lPb2JqdYRBhV6GFaTfR-Ir9ZHwcLIBi9P6R2zM_7vys9TcLdwv86-wyLdxNtIshHon8-Cqdzw4RjmyOT4qypOpUnQprJEU8fSy66ovDgSJrKr0kQEUpYYmk20nMPolIr1orHyzKo_BT19vCUmkOwe_R99q7rXBRa9RNPjPmYx8Bt1TkV-_VvwooUc8Cc7qlyoKpvd7depPRNjgbqbZiRPe41B1Z0cYHP3x7HKxFhpP0uzj44pxCEtxT9devMWcJVZBposytadd_t2LaLhZnNj3EgsD0zn9Mad67cBxdHBq3VKLr2Zn_9gAbXnJ6yiAdC0W-yfZlfKmqQ%3D%3D)
**I had to grow up fast, Peter,_and I learned many life lessons at an early age._**

After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney.

Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq.

[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG65deU9ACd2HDdg2ggNkIUg5ua5X5cfMLJVVxfqyOMGFC1B2sshQso6lPb2JqdYRBhV6GFaTfR-Ir9ZHwcLIBi9P6R2zM_7vys9TcLdwv86-wyLdxNtIshHon8-Cqdzw4RjmyOT4qypOpUnQprJEU8fSy66ovDgSJrKr0kQEUpYYmk20nMPolIr1orHyzKo_BT19vCUmkOwe_R99q7rXBRa9V18gkKZvMhlJMjpcq5qbMd6uhTv6SUkHmWEijraGgRqmxXmX4nXLUI7zeAKTyHesjSplAjtADMC8Z-APfTnQJBkhcxaK8aip2k6y9Inlb6pZISmKApv_DpOu7yPXraHLON8gx6YwluTBE7gl_A3_K7MIOhbfKwsqYie-GJVbdUg%3D%3D)
Peter, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility.

But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need.

**It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on.**

**We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from.**

Peter, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families.

**[That's why I'm reaching out with a simple request: if you have a few dollars to spare, would you consider making a contribution to support my campaign by clicking this link or the button below?](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG65deU9ACd2HDdg2ggNkIUg5ua5X5cfMLJVVxfqyOMGFC1B2sshQso6lPb2JqdYRBhV6GFaTfR-Ir9ZHwcLIBi9P6R2zM_7vys9TcLdwv86-wyLdxNtIshHon8-Cqdzw4RjmyOT4qypOpUnQprJEU8fSy66ovDgSJrKr0kQEUpYYmk20nMPolIr1orHyzKo_BT19vCUmkOwe_R99q7rXBRa-9PkxjigKR9ae4HQnn5x4xKjlK9lZQ8qaF6XuvtLQoUty_tm1tHVYUH6-a1fi3GJ2Rfq0NNHbPxDNbvbJiPLugQjuF3xsw92sanstpHji5iDhOyHKL6V7xfTZJtZK177kqayojs1VH74O8p_o7lMGFwe3CKMH51DtRZtEyX-oajw%3D%3D)**

| [** CONTRIBUTE HERE **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG65deU9ACd2HDdg2ggNkIUg5ua5X5cfMLJVVxfqyOMGFC1B2sshQso6lPb2JqdYRBhV6GFaTfR-Ir9ZHwcLIBi9P6R2zM_7vys9TcLdwv86-wyLdxNtIshHon8-Cqdzw4RjmyOT4qypOpUnQprJEU8fSy66ovDgSJrKr0kQEUpYYmk20nMPolIr1orHyzKo_BT19vCUmkOwe_R99q7rXBRa9FSvp4l6_kARpt26aPhiRTDS7WLBg1zrjwpqqGoEDw3P3FZt_R0GjLx4ySsvOE04N1zi_mYQIegRw-lQUQUc1xZfHJ-qZTwFXdSDIpp2V3rhAw7vtVx-wrNZx4IGgKrqH1RY2kNFsgkdd77mTfqFEltbZJIojTtAa47slzjMV1Fw%3D%3D)
---

If you're unable to contribute financially, I understand. I'd greatly appreciate it if you could forward this email along to a friend or family member to help amplify my message.

Together, I know we can build the country that protects our communities, rewards hard work, and ensures every hard-working American has a shot at their dreams, just like I was so fortunate to have.

Thank you for taking a moment out of your busy day to read this message.

Sincerely,
Lindsey Graham

[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG61jqCsxA93Zi5P0wXsMwNYWrvAF3SHYcRsPor7bvlAQMF4PuT26u5EqEeQoLnrAfSLS2ntvfg02X4KNRccWRMaOTDTcuBhRpdE9a4T0EE_L9JfzVdFjgvRklbkhgl08J0VnPF-KM5LGAvgdJWEPwjOW4EaLkoZtIOCZ8HT-r48Z9hzg489jj01-FEc6VrsliRkTmnVBG1U92uc9K1EUlYwE%3D)

[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=340904a0c5ee42e7bbfa8082e036cffd&_e=ueWIpJpgfvbbzMp0NnmG65deU9ACd2HDdg2ggNkIUg5ua5X5cfMLJVVxfqyOMGFC1B2sshQso6lPb2JqdYRBhV6GFaTfR-Ir9ZHwcLIBi9P6R2zM_7vys9TcLdwv86-wyLdxNtIshHon8-Cqdzw4RjmyOT4qypOpUnQprJEU8fSy66ovDgSJrKr0kQEUpYYmk20nMPolIr1orHyzKo_BT19vCUmkOwe_R99q7rXBRa-iUorx9mUbstXzd0R7r10ZD8guqPtaPIUbaAWmOhS2GKwK3ofrV4MZsTOy-hymKpWrvDZ4NwDtJ1P_H-K9vg_J9GuvcZuFw9H9JHfzJWb3Z2GLDCism8fGZU3276hXEhUpVy5en9Hkms9EXOb-ynehyLApphFWDMudVUbF0DXRIw%3D%3D)
In the United States Senate, South Carolina's Lindsey Graham has earned a reputation as a conservative, a problem-solver, and a leader who gets things done. He is a great friend to our men and women serving in uniform, a respected voice on national security matters, a leading advocate for cutting the out-of-control spending in Washington, and a champion for confirming conservative judges.

name: Bobby Jindal
subject: NEWSWEEK Op-Ed: RFK Is Right—Food Additive Transparency Can Make America Healthy Again | Opinion
body:
| | | |
---
[](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=e941b34996d94bf48ab9841e0fe1f5ef&_e=4_jWcwH7xeGotljgoBMKGDTWgvBeKyIPeJlf4yTgZCybA08_U2JpYsWzsfX9zrWasngLFhBbTpq4rKLpfe5FLeu49k9QZW33OeDCd-O8AMbHzEVBpEwBTpEB0P6VAYDO_SgDzMQ27uBjEP-GLTyxjAURuyzpHjHSB_rbzJJOitBTxEG-6V6WLZu1Jt28xpm1VfrfdToqzNxkPY3SRIf9EQ%3D%3D)
---
RFK Is Right—Food Additive Transparency Can Make America Healthy Again[](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=e941b34996d94bf48ab9841e0fe1f5ef&_e=4_jWcwH7xeGotljgoBMKGNDnZiF0_D6ziABp5FbAFPqCJiwedVGRw9oXDUkHFNVrpjYpGqmU9jNEtgWy37__x5fh0UyCg20denMWa70CQYm-CYkaZydo1K6AXYjticSwVdo8agzqVhvofiBBUl4B_ijyer0ROg3qROjkUwbmewkFeXYWFU26UAY6mhML5eQwIeCluspakhh2e0Nf0THYxYt_HgF8C8UVSBogrPSCitc%3D "View More Articles By Bobby Jindal And Charlie Katebi")Bobby Jindal and Charlie Katebi
---
In a decisive move to protect public health, Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is phasing out several petroleum-based food dyes from American foods. Kennedy should build on this measure by also closing a longstanding loophole that allows food companies to quietly flood grocery stores with hundreds of unregulated additives.

In 1958, a new provision to the nation's food safety laws required food manufacturers to request approval from the FDA before they include a newly developed additive in their products. However, the same provision created an alternative pathway, allowing manufacturers to insert new additives without FDA oversight if the ingredient is "generally recognized as safe" (GRAS) by food scientists. Companies that label an additive as GRAS may inform the FDA of its use in their products, but they have no legal responsibility to do so.

Congress originally intended the GRAS pathway for widely known ingredients such as nutmeg and paprika. But large food manufacturers have exploited this loophole, flooding the market with new industrial additives—without proof they're safe for consumers. A 2011 study estimated that, since 1997, 1,000 additives were inserted into the American diet without FDA review.

Federal law requires food companies to contract with scientists to evaluate GRAS-labeled additives for potential health risks, but conflicts of interest abound. A 2013 analysis found 22 percent of GRAS-ingredient safety evaluations were made by an employee of the product's manufacturer; another 13 percent were conducted by a consulting firm selected by the manufacturer. **[Finish reading on Newsweek.com](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=e941b34996d94bf48ab9841e0fe1f5ef&_e=4_jWcwH7xeGotljgoBMKGIzn8nKzBtS_qSnnSNsYdWL-h-8-NmNnfFqv0YMZ3nvDIHM-MbDnV71xegUq03FKV_gd6pfTwy3oDENxlq9drC9aKWyFGlbcICCbWemRDPZSd8G2B14jc-QoBdlNh4FcK9kEGtr0ArPMubkYUphrB-ZVsIkD8sK_VC0H2AKxRcAoW3if37qgBBjBoftgcuDcm5JAtjk89qgRCZmCv1sGAwpa-Rs0yghYKZuy__LoMEHCTwKCDZ1fwLWcaz-gK9tgZYT0S2YQX2pIRVKFsB2YvJ4qLflA577Rdk5Zm9ZQNjPe)**
---

name: Coach Tuberville
subject: You said you were in

name: Michael Bennet
subject: I have a story I wanted to share.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q2-g?refcode=X_250520_accidentalsenator_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q2-g?refcode=X_250520_accidentalsenator_act]
Hi folks,
I want to tell you a little more about why I’m running for governor.
Colorado is the best state in the country. But everywhere I go, Coloradans tell me that no matter how hard they work, they can’t get ahead and their kids can’t get ahead.
Our economy is working really well for the people at the top, but it’s broken for just about everyone else.
I’m no stranger to fixing broken systems.
Before my life in public service, I spent years in the private sector turning around failing businesses. Then, I went to work for the new mayor of Denver—you now know him as Senator John Hickenlooper—and together, we fixed the city’s longstanding budget crisis.
Shortly thereafter, I was selected to become the superintendent of the Denver Public Schools—one of the best jobs I ever had. When I became superintendent, Denver’s academic achievement was among the worst in the state. Over the next five years, I worked hand-in-hand with teachers, principals, and community leaders to transform the district into one of the highest-performing in Colorado.
In 2009, I was appointed to fill Colorado’s U.S. Senate seat. I wasn’t an obvious choice to become a senator. Only 3% of Coloradans knew who I was. Republicans called me the "accidental Senator." A national political website reacted with the headline "WTF?!!"
But, I went to the Senate because I knew that I would have the chance to provide Coloradans with more opportunity to get ahead . In the last 16 years, that’s precisely what we’ve done.
We passed bills to:
- Expand the Child Tax Credit and pass one of the largest tax cuts for working families in a generation
- Invest in our infrastructure and create thousands of good-paying jobs in Colorado
- Deliver high-speed internet to urban and rural communities across our state
- Combat climate change and support our transition to a clean energy economy
- Lower the cost of prescription drugs for our seniors
Now, I’m running for Governor because I know the most effective way to address the economic stress Coloradans face will be from right here at home.
Working together, we can bring down the cost of housing, healthcare, and childcare, revitalize our education system, and build a thriving economy that works for everyone, not just the people at the top.
So, friend, what do you say? If you’re ready to join our campaign, please consider chipping in whatever you can today. >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q2-g?refcode=X_250520_accidentalsenator_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q2-g?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=X_250520_accidentalsenator_act] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q2-g?express_lane_other=true&refcode=X_250520_accidentalsenator_act]
Your support is what we’re counting on to power this campaign forward.
Thanks,
Michael

name: Katrina @ API PA
subject: Happy Election Day Pennsylvania!
body:
https://www.apipennsylvania.org [https://www.apipennsylvania.org]
Hi Peter,
I remember one of the first times I voted. I finished my shift at one of my jobs at 7:30 PM. I missed the bus and decided to run from my workplace to my polling place. I arrived sweaty and out of breath, but luckily just before my polling place closed, and I was able to vote. I felt so relieved that even though my life was busy and stressful, I was still able to have a voice in the future of my community.
We’ve been talking to Asian Americans from Pittsburgh to Philadelphia and everywhere in between about the future they want to build for their communities. People want affordable housing, reliable public transportation, good schools for their kids, and to live in neighborhoods where everyone is welcome and where our immigrant communities are supported and safe. Today, many of us have the power to vote and to elect leaders who will care for our communities just as much as we do.
If you still have your mail-in ballot, you need to return it to a drop box or your local election office by 8 PM. If you are voting in-person this year, polls will close at 8 PM! Hopefully, you’ll be in less of a rush than I was though. If you have any questions about voting, reach out to the election protection hotline at 888-API-VOTE. [https://866ourvote.org/]
Return Your Mail-In Ballot [https://vote.pa/return/?org=apipa] Find Your Polling Place [https://www.pavoterservices.pa.gov/Pages/PollingPlaceInfo.aspx]
Happy Election Day!
Katrina Eames
Digital Manager
Asian Pacific Islander Political Alliance (API PA)
https://www.facebook.com/apipennsylvania [https://www.facebook.com/apipennsylvania] https://twitter.com/apipennsylvania [https://twitter.com/apipennsylvania] https://instagram.com/apipennsylvania [https://instagram.com/apipennsylvania] https://www.tiktok.com/@apipennsylvania [https://www.tiktok.com/@apipennsylvania] https://bsky.app/profile/apipennsylvania.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/apipennsylvania.bsky.social]

API PA
PO Box 22611
Philadelphia, PA 19110
United States

Justice Department Launches Investigation Into Chicago Mayor Johnson’s Employment Practices (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/05/justice-department-launches-investigation-into-chicago-mayor-johnsons-employment-practices.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=30b170f26c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/05/justice-department-launches-investigation-into-chicago-mayor-johnsons-employment-practices.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=30b170f26c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson. (NBC 5 Chicago video clip)
By Illinois Review

On Monday, the U.S. Department of Justice opened an investigation into Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson’s hiring practices after he delivered remarks at the Apostolic Church of God in Woodlawn on Sunday emphasizing the race of his top hires within his administration.

“Business and Economic Neighborhood Development, the deputy mayor is a Black woman.”

“Department of Planning and Development is a Black woman.”

“Infastructure, deputy mayor is a Black woman.”

“Chief of Operations is a Black man.”

“Budget Director is a Black woman.”

“Senior Advisor is a Black man.”

The comments drew the immediate attention of Assistant Attorney General of the Civil Rights Division Harmeet Dhilllon, who penned a letter on Monday informing the mayor of the investigation.

Illinois Review is the largest conservative media source in Illinois, and the only source in Illinois that has been shared by President Donald Trump. Twitter () Facebook () IllinoisReview.com () Justice Department Launches Investigation Into Chicago Mayor Johnson’s Employment Practices () Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson. (NBC 5 Chicago video clip) By Illinois Review On Monday, the U.S. Department of Justice opened an investigation into Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson’s hiring practices after he delivered remarks at the Apostolic Church of God in Woodlawn on Sunday emphasizing the race of his top hires within his administration. “Business and Economic Neighborhood Development, the deputy mayor is a Black woman.” “Department of Planning and Development is a Black woman.” “Infastructure, deputy mayor is a Black woman.” “Chief of Operations is a Black man.” “Budget Director is a Black woman.” “Senior Advisor is a Black man.” The comments drew the immediate attention of Assistant Attorney General of the Civil Rights Division Harmeet Dhilllon, who penned a letter on Monday informing the mayor of the investigation.
